## Abbott Threatens $200M Cut Over ICE Policies
Gov. Greg Abbott [threatened](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDGu2zAMQE8jbwwk2rHiwUMXbb9rR0O2KIetLAUyf5zcvnCAnqAzwcfH9-tr-vKcOK8__UajvGKZMh17IhGqEy_0KIkXpv07B6qRKwnn9zyhxh50B-Y2Bc_pPXVNGGcdBtPQaKztr0Yb0zb3cca5XyjEdhi6OcSIdjDRLtbeommvi2l4PGG6MzdjtLni5aZ174PH2IZhtiaqTlNgKfUidxJ6-T2md3lSvSxla9J4F3nsqv2h0Cl0x3Fc_uxezqFCd36j0H3WFLrzlEKnO4XOWIVuLU9YK63g57mIgNwreaG8A2oNG6fEJQNniN85cF4h1rLB5n-XCh8oLCxMO5xCwAvBv2IKXVPH8DhOxt5b1el185w-1vJ_rZ8j_g0AAP__X12UgQ) to pull a combined total of about $200 million in state funding from Houston, Dallas, and Austin over their ICE cooperation policies, sending letters to the cities' respective mayors this week. Houston stands to lose $110 million in public safety grants, [Dallas](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkD2yozAQhE8DmShJiL9AwSZk3nRDaoQGmFohudDYmNu_wld4WQfdXd3fv8f0AAoU17-wo-XPkqaIZw7IjMdEMz5ToJkwv6LHY6EDmeLlJi11K6QRqp88ULgmU3rrpB9UiVZ1XdsoqVRdbhZU7WTTu3rQrjdN3_hZ4zArCX3nDfqS7F0mjeqVkqrRVS9lCx70UvvBdWopjERPnI6KN2T8QF7Cld54VHPay2A35mcu6j-FHgs9nudZLelj7hu3odDjLQs9ruktwLnELHg7EBhjFhfE_2KnECjFLJZX9BRX4SEEyMInzJHFvEFcUdCM4ovjKg_rn-cdym1XGLnuQOG7hn_H8G31TwAAAP__Fl2JnA) nearly $90 million, and Austin about $2.5 million if the governor's demands aren't met. Abbott cited a city ordinance in Houston and a recently adopted Austin Police Department policy that limits cooperation between the police department and Homeland Security in detaining illegal immigrants. Similarly, the governor said a General Order from Dallas PD contradicts the city's promise to "fully participate" in ICE efforts. Houston officials [paused crucial votes](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2u4yAQhZ8Gd1gDJv4pKLZxl223tDAM8UjERDCJ47dfJbpvcNujo3P0ff-uy9VRov32193R8jvmZcejJmTGspDHR07kCetzD1giFWTaz3XRoHsJRqpxCY7SuZgm2BXCpBq0ahj6iwKlumazY3DdCrpbw9oNo9cmevAweTNNE_hL35D9jIFRo1KgLrodAXoXnI5dmNZBRWEAA3EuLW_I-HY1pjO_sLQ-35tkN-ZHFd0foWeh5-M42pjfut_ys3LePyWh5w-V0PNPKDdXClXp83PnU5JH-QU9ZXKMlZtiw-OglKj2gzBwuztK3zv-naSX1f8DAAD__wmDfgo) on updates to county ICE cooperation policies amid the pressure from Abbott, while Dallas said it would comply with all state and federal laws and respond by the governor's deadline. Austin Mayor Kirk Watson indicated that no adjustments would be made to their recent policy changes.
## Texas Needs $174B for Future Water Demand
The Texas Water Development Board (TWDB) is estimating [$174 billion](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL3O6yAMQJ-GbKlswpefgeEu2XrXO0YETINEQgRu0779FX2Fb_bx0bH_3Ze7CTEcj79mJ81vn5aDrhKJmfISLJ0pBhuoPA9H2YdMHI7PukiQfQuqxXFxJsTPohqnV3ATNqRxGPofBMSu2bSHaQTv7OSssuSHtSO0qu86GNEOTjZBVxkoHBEBf-RtBOiNM9J3bloH9EIBucAp33gjprcpPn7Si_LNpr2JemM-i-j-CDkLOZeTLOfnHpM1sd5SKSFnftdhevLWfh0txfY0JQk5V0rIuWYIOYMScsahrlxubS_DlNszmqPJ2p1XiDGUfhAKHrsJ8dvAv_vcS8v_AQAA__-ov4K3) in costs to avoid a future water crisis in the state, according to a draft of its 2027 state water plan released Thursday. The $174 billion figure would cover 3,000 water management strategy projects, including designating water reservoir sites and consolidating and resolving interregional water initiatives. The board predicted that, should its proposed strategies not be implemented, a severe drought could cost the state $91 billion in economic damages by 2030 and $177 billion in damages per year by 2080. Texas' [data center boom](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDGu4yAURVeDOyzANuCCYhp3mXZKi8Cz_SQCFrzEye5HP1v49b06Ojr_buvNY8K8__UPcPTeyprhagmIoK4Y4CwJA0J75gh1wwqE-XNflVCai5FLu0aP6bOOXXR3EWfZgZPG6EkKKYfucNareTJGm3FQswigw2QHq7VVxm4Qpg7dD0yM0kop5KR6K4T20attiPPdyI2NAiJSqT0dQPD2bUuf8oLah_LokjuIzsaGP0wtTC3XdfXfUyOfo6-xL3VnamlUKkJjavmuvJEn4JcnqPxMPvNQGnHMoYJvwEOp57PxcFRshDzW8twPYmrpqovnhSlh04aNYn94TF8T-l2_l1P_AwAA__9NBYeJ) and population growth, combined with ongoing drought conditions, are the primary culprits in the water development board's hiking its projected costs from $80 billion to $174 billion in just four years.
## Texas Cities Among Best in U.S. for New Grads
Texas is an attractive destination for [new college graduates](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0L3uozAQBPCnMR3IuziYFC6uocu1VyJ_rJOVHIjsvZC8_Yl7hX890vw08-e23jwX3u6__ZOcfPK-bnS0QiJUV4702gtHpvZ3S1QzVxLevmFFjVOvTQ_zmjyX72q65IJOV-jIgbXTBTTA2D1ctrOnfI2IebIWo00QwkhgApoUo-_YnWXawAyg4YLDrPXkk8c8pmuwkJXRlFj2OsiDhD6-5fLd31SHuD-74h4ir6bGXwoXhctxHEOllHk7Y4XLuUfhEqhJH1mYWl8p0ib9vfrU-lNXuHTVpdfBpXCbrDL6_vRc_gvys1_eDv8FAAD__5qod_A) to land jobs, according to a ranking from real estate site Redfin and the Glassdoor job community. The study ranked the top 10 big, mid-sized, and small cities for new grads based on average starting salaries and the affordability of starter homes, with Texas grabbing four spots across the board. Dallas earned No. 4 on the big-city list, along with Houston and Austin at No. 6 and No. 10, respectively. Waco ranked ninth on the mid-sized list. Washington D.C., New Orleans, and Springfield, Ill. all received No. 1 rankings. clean_body: Gov. Greg Abbott to pull a combined total of about $200 million in state funding from Houston, Dallas, and Austin over their ICE cooperation policies, sending letters to the cities' respective mayors on Thursday. | | | | | | Saturday, April 18, 2026 ---|--- Good Morning! On this day in 1934, entrepreneur C.A. Tannahill opened the country's in Fort Worth. The "Wash-a-teria" contained four electric washing machines that were rented out by the hour. Buc-ee's is closing one of its Texas locations. It's a big gas station, but not a gas station, if you know what we mean. Read the story in Around Texas. One year ago, President Trump called it "a Great Honor for our Country" when an American was elected Pope for the first time in history, then, earlier this month, posted that "if I wasn't in the White House, Leo wouldn't be in the Vatican." Now Trump is calling Pope Leo "WEAK on Crime," and Pope Leo is firing back: "I have no fear of the Trump administration." This isn't a diplomatic spat. This is an open feud between the President of the United States and the leader of 1.4 billion Catholics. Only on today's episode of The Flyover Podcast , Ayla Brown shares an exclusive breakdown of what's been said and how we got here. Today's sponsor, , offers a simple, drug-free light-therapy device designed to target stubborn nail fungus at the source and help restore clearer, healthier nails from the comfort of home. Abilene ☁️ 74/51° | Amarillo ☀️ 62/39° | Austin 🌤️ 74/53° | Brownsville ☁️ 85/74° | Corpus Christi 🌧️ 83/66° | DFW 🌦️ 75/54° | El Paso ☀️ 77/55° | Houston 🌤️ 83/63° | Laredo 🌤️ 90/59° | Lubbock ☁️ 70/46° | Midland ☁️ 74/51° | San Antonio 🌤️ 77/56° | Tyler 🌧️ 72/54° | Waco 🌧️ 72/53° Take The Flyover with you. Busy day? Listen to our daily 15-minute podcast while commuting, cooking, or walking the dog--your Flyover fix, anywhere. | | Abbott Threatens $200M Cut Over ICE Policies Gov. Greg Abbott to pull a combined total of about $200 million in state funding from Houston, Dallas, and Austin over their ICE cooperation policies, sending letters to the cities' respective mayors this week. Houston stands to lose $110 million in public safety grants, nearly $90 million, and Austin about $2.5 million if the governor's demands aren't met. Abbott cited a city ordinance in Houston and a recently adopted Austin Police Department policy that limits cooperation between the police department and Homeland Security in detaining illegal immigrants. Similarly, the governor said a General Order from Dallas PD contradicts the city's promise to "fully participate" in ICE efforts. Houston officials on updates to county ICE cooperation policies amid the pressure from Abbott, while Dallas said it would comply with all state and federal laws and respond by the governor's deadline. Austin Mayor Kirk Watson indicated that no adjustments would be made to their recent policy changes. Texas Needs $174B for Future Water Demand The Texas Water Development Board (TWDB) is estimating in costs to avoid a future water crisis in the state, according to a draft of its 2027 state water plan released Thursday. The $174 billion figure would cover 3,000 water management strategy projects, including designating water reservoir sites and consolidating and resolving interregional water initiatives. The board predicted that, should its proposed strategies not be implemented, a severe drought could cost the state $91 billion in economic damages by 2030 and $177 billion in damages per year by 2080. Texas' and population growth, combined with ongoing drought conditions, are the primary culprits in the water development board's hiking its projected costs from $80 billion to $174 billion in just four years. Texas Cities Among Best in U.S. for New Grads Texas is an attractive destination for to land jobs, according to a ranking from real estate site Redfin and the Glassdoor job community. The study ranked the top 10 big, mid-sized, and small cities for new grads based on average starting salaries and the affordability of starter homes, with Texas grabbing four spots across the board. Dallas earned No. 4 on the big-city list, along with Houston and Austin at No. 6 and No. 10, respectively. Waco ranked ninth on the mid-sized list. Washington D.C., New Orleans, and Springfield, Ill. all received No. 1 rankings. You can see the . | Flying together with our sponsor The Last Foot Fungus Treatment You 'll Ever Need Struggling with stubborn nail fungus or discolored nails? to target fungal infections deep beneath the nail surface--where creams often fail. This drug-free, non-invasive treatment helps eliminate fungus at the source while supporting healthier nail regrowth and improved nail strength over time. With quick, painless sessions you can do at home, while promoting better nail hygiene and long-term protection from recurring infections. | ➤ Statewide: U.S. Senate hopeful James Talarico's campaign raised $27 million in the last quarter, outpacing his GOP counterparts. Meanwhile, Sen. John Cornyn reported about $9 million raised compared to runoff competitor Ken Paxton's $2.2 million. () ➤ Dallas: A federal jury convicted Donald McCray of sending threatening letters to judges in Texas and New York, as well as for a biological weapons hoax against a federal courthouse in Fort Worth. () ➤ Edinburg: U.S. Agriculture Secretary Brooke Rollins and U.S. Sen. John Cornyn attended the groundbreaking of a sterile fly production facility in South Texas yesterday. The facility is designed to mitigate the spread of screwworms, which threaten the health of livestock. () ➤ McKinney: Paralympic gold medalist Dory Selinger was killed Thursday in a cycling accident in which he was struck by a vehicle. A friend said of Selinger: " If you knew him, you would swear he loved you because he did. I loved him with my whole heart. And I'm aching immensely." () ➤ Statewide: Four Texas cities--Edinburg, Georgetown, Conroe, and Grand Prairie--ranked in the top 20 for highest property taxes nationwide, according to a new study. No Texas city was included in the top 10. () ➤ Port Lavaca: One of Texas' smallest Buc-ee's is closing down after 26 years and will be replaced by a 7-Eleven with a Laredo Taco shop. The location is a plus-size gas station, but not the mega-size that is a Buc-ee's trademark. () Enjoy reading The Texas Flyover?mailto:?cc=editor@thetexasflyover.com&subject=My%20new%20favorite%20newsletter!&body=Hey!%0A%0AI%E2%80%99ve%20been%20reading%20a%20new%20newsletter%20every%20morning%20called%20The%20Texas%20Flyover%2C%20and%20I%20think%20you%20would%20like%20it%20a%20lot.%0A%0AIt%20covers%20a%20wide%20range%20of%20Texas%20news%2C%20and%20it%20doesn't%20have%20the%20bias%20that%20most%20media%20coverage%20is%20full%20of.%0A%0AI%20think%20you%20should%20give%20it%20a%20try.%0A%0AJust%20copy%20this%20link%20and%20paste%20it%20into%20your%20browser%3A%20https%3A%2F%2Fthetexasflyover.com%2F%3Futm%3DDL%0A%0ALet%20me%20know%20if%20you%20like%20it.%20I%20think%20you%20will! to share with your friends and family. | ➤ Will Anderson Jr. signed a three-year, $150 million contract extension with the Houston Texans, making him the highest-paid non-quarterback in NFL history. () ➤ The Houston Rockets take on the LA Lakers tonight in Los Angeles in their first game of the NBA Playoffs. () ➤ Meanwhile, the Dallas Stars play the Minnesota Wild tonight in their first NHL postseason matchup of the year. () ➤ Dallas Mavericks rookie Cooper Flagg ranked ninth on the NBA's top-selling jersey list for the 2025-26 regular season. () ➤ Yesterday's Results: | | | | | Flying together with our sponsor ➤ Millions are finding fast relief from foot pain, without pills or costly treatments. , used just minutes a day, boosts circulation and eases tingling, burning, and stabbing discomfort. If you struggle with plantar fasciitis or neuropathy, don't miss this breakthrough before it sells out again. () | Courtesy via X. | Flying together with our sponsor Embarrassing Toenails? This Breakthrough Gadget Is Fixing Them Tired of thick, yellow, stubborn nail fungus that never seems to go away? blasts fungus at the source... fast, pain-free, and right from home. No creams. No pills. Just visibly clearer, healthier nails in weeks, not months. | ➤ Designs have been released for a surf community in Texas that would include 88 3D-printed houses and two artificial surf lagoons. () ➤ Texas A&M researchers have developed a nasal spray that was shown to reverse brain aging in preclinical animal trials by neutralizing the inflammation that leads to brain fog and memory issues. () ➤ A new billboard promoting pet adoption in Dallas honors a golden retriever named Kensington Campbell, who had 120,000 Instagram followers. Kensington passed away recently from cancer. () Flying together with our sponsor ➤ The Weight-Loss Patch everyone is suddenly talking about. What if weight loss didn't require injections, strict diets, or complicated routines? is getting attention for a surprisingly simple idea: a small patch designed to help curb cravings, support metabolism, and make staying on track feel easier. Curious how it works and why so many people are trying it? See the full details here. () | Do you own a bicycle? 1. 2. Yesterday's Results: Would you try crawfish-flavored ice cream? 1. No: 78% 2. Yes: 14% 3. Already have: 8% | In what Texas suburb was the 1974 indie production Benji predominantly filmed? ** | (Step One) The Texas Flyover 1942 W Gray St Unit #158 Houston, TX 77019 | ---|---

name: Jen Kiggans
subject: From Navy helicopter pilot to Congress
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kiggans for Congress
body:
I am about to ask you what I know is no small favor.

| Peter,
I know your inbox is probably overflowing right now with politicians begging for your hard-earned money using every scare tactic in the book.
I truly hate having to send messages like this, but I really appreciate your time!
My last message didn't get the response I was hoping for, and now I'm starting to feel like we're falling behind. We can't keep fighting if we don't have the resources to keep the lights on and pay my amazing staff.
Let me take a second to re-introduce myself: My name is Jen Kiggans.
I am a former Navy helicopter pilot. I flew H-46s and H-3s in the Persian Gulf. After my time in the military, I used my GI Bill benefits to go back to school and become a board-certified Geriatric Nurse Practitioner. I am married to a retired Navy F/A-18 pilot, and now I am a proud Navy Mom.
---
[](https://trk.emails.dailygopnews.)
---
And that is exactly WHY I ran for Congress in 2022.
I knew that I needed to step up and do my part to make sure that my four kids inherit a GREAT America!
Against all odds, we flipped Virginia's 2nd District RED in one of the tightest, must-win races in the entire country. But elections have serious consequences and these radical Left Democrats are not going to give up without a fight.
Now, National Democrats and Left-leaning Super PACs are raking in millions to attack me and take back the House this year. They want to turn our great commonwealth into California AND my far-Left opponent JUST announced she raised over $1.75 MILLION in the first quarter alone.
They will stop at absolutely nothing to win.
So today, I'm recruiting YOU to join my mission to save our country from the Democrats' grasp once and for all. That is why I am about to ask you what I know is no small favor. [Will you chip in just a few dollars to support a former Navy pilot on this critical mission to SAVE OUR GOP HOUSE MAJORITY?](https://trk.emails.dailygopnews.)
---
| [JOIN JEN'S MISSION >>](https://trk.emails.dailygopnews.)
---
| [CHIP IN RIGHT NOW!](https://trk.emails.dailygopnews.-_Dhxn4rWgUD8C9_Q)
---
As a Navy Vet, I've never turned away from a tough fight, and trust me, I won't start now. But to keep our House Majority intact and continue fighting for our country's future, I really need your support!
Thank you all so very, very much!
\- Jen Kiggans

---
| Paid for by Kiggans for Congress

---
Use of military images or rank does not imply endorsement by the U.S. Department of War or the Navy

P.O. Box 5042
Virginia Beach, VA 23471
[Privacy Policy](https://trk.emails.dailygopnews.com/c/7/eyJhaSI6OTE4ODYyNjYsImUiOiJtZy1lbmM6NWh6RDRoYmhSOTR0SVViRGFVQXlzUGowaHcxSkVGa0EiLCJyaSI6IjIwNjgtZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iLCJycSI6IjAyLWIyNjEwOC0xOWI1NzUzODU5ODc0MDRmOGViNDk0YWU1YzViNDY5ZCIsIm0iOmZhbHNlLCJ1aSI6IjQiLCJ1biI6IiIsInUiOiJodHRwczovL2plbmZvcmNvbmdyZXNzLmNvbS9wcml2YWN5LXBvbGljeS8_cmVjdXJyaW5nPXRydWUmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPTIwMjYwNDE4X1dNLVA3LjExNDUxMF90MTg3NDQxMi0yMDY4JmV4X3RpZD0yMDI2MDQxOF9XTS1QNy4xMTQ1MTBfdDE4NzQ0MTItMjA2OCZ4aWQ9VjEuSldlTzdHV3dqQTBPYUxjQjNBWmlYU3I1end4RVJ5MkxRMDB6VDJmUmxkUDB1b19VVU9McTRpTVFScXF2N1lXRyJ9/T4W33Zsi0R31y052qHRBQw)

name: Gary Sinise, American Veterans Center
subject: As American turns 250, this may be our last chance to honor them

The biggest National Memorial Day Parade yet | | | [](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=59227a9cc4c34343b25228e6fdd3eea2&_e=qZkR5r4NLP0mGVx0Yv42entrTvtbOyqvo3ogmPKnJbCB0VdsVv-TWU990rm3YHDCbXXr4CpooZNsLzKUMwzbohLwlRUjHZpZhDbWppKaczCXBzxWsWSv-GlvhK0hLVx5ZE10MipE58dbiHEuLBmHLU6Q7jWje6cuS8Z9lHJBWX8mPn4OfRbcw4EACzCTWa6azwhJq-AXSILw9R9diUtmCJQMMgZ62J7_vNK3Y8rJpwyacCAaGp-wR3b38tBq-OzCLguxp8wsjSqJAPJEae9SOBhVyeEu2Zc3mMWZ7elmn2A22K_i89ea7MFndhelK56kOUhE8f1g8CiEB3GXX23vq-fUHLheybaLClgHehW83CQPZ7BR_mkrH8QBLtRL1UhO7UHHM9wY-lW7uSjS1dMAiSbm_rI_EtOIGoneUCKcER2O5oWVjOCmG4mru84kJ7f57Z-EQOkf56JKzGBwuiFgWGIG5rUuQvEcSvR1PEKnoZWU10toEWW9RkpMcW8hV9mk92VhGWD2M8JRLcl0tb62zf8hmrRTj5wbgrfGN0IAPpoKOXUPa939aDsJ4eKyLR-UQ2Nrvh9-pg3iYwxyBIj49HPNlVt7B7fqTKJ-c8EKLgvfcA6XWrqSa2NuuTvecfUf5MuH2tkXo9sor7lx11YJjbQpeykDvIWMY1DDYyeNidNbQHoGi1W6W7iVdftN1Avg)
---
Patriot,
---
It's Gary Sinise here. You may know me from my roles in movies like Apollo 13 _ _ and The Green Mile or the CBS television series CSI: NY - but it was in 1994 playing the role of Lt. Dan in Forrest Gump that changed my life and inspired my enduring connection to our defenders, veterans, and first responders. I've served as an Honorary Grand Marshal for the National Memorial Day Parade in Washington, D.C. nearly every year since American Veterans Center revived it in 2005. **But I've never been more excited to serve as an Honorary Grand Marshall than I am this year - the year of America's 250****th**** birthday.**
---
[](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=59227a9cc4c34343b25228e6fdd3eea2&_e=qZkR5r4NLP0mGVx0Yv42entrTvtbOyqvo3ogmPKnJbCB0VdsVv-TWU990rm3YHDCbXXr4CpooZNsLzKUMwzbohLwlRUjHZpZhDbWppKaczCXBzxWsWSv-GlvhK0hLVx5ZE10MipE58dbiHEuLBmHLU6Q7jWje6cuS8Z9lHJBWX8mPn4OfRbcw4EACzCTWa6azwhJq-AXSILw9R9diUtmCJQMMgZ62J7_vNK3Y8rJpwyacCAaGp-wR3b38tBq-OzC30LCXPco1iOgh56Fb1LyJmCjb4TMp8Z_9EQYO19sW6FuwDjlBVW1D3tWfZQ-tSwWYflHVCxsCzL102wProWKvckyJeGTCCMyPBeJGNx7sD9Yvj5HIT04fE5cxxTzoQV7u5ukSeIgVIAsr7C5SccowBqdtkgJTc9xKSW04EiDwyqSqzcWEkZnIzQYM3eDHziGHqntruacNmMsaAJtoRei6epLabSxs5n_aNudO84IBTNeU7L_kIXmAN7xLsow57_nhzTQEgrtNQTM8SA3tYoUhKYFp0EbDnHheG1Gm1htJ1dES_iO8ClZhCKEINVz0FoFeFYx9cDDEOCtRqN-G8Iu7LaoC1k5D2OKSbH2WHisn_Y-izemMbvsUT0ve_QifQL4AhvkfaikoKNKDI4FaAmqUnP4SIfR0y26nTirU21utxb-EudItbDM2DeYvhEvbPgk)
---
Patriot, this Memorial Day marks the first major national act of remembrance of the veterans who made the ultimate sacrifice to defend America from its enemies and ensure it has remained the Land of the Free for the last 250 years. And this year's National Memorial Day Parade will be a special edition - the biggest parade yet - where national memory meets living history as veterans from every military generation celebrate America and honor the service and sacrifice of their fallen brethren. **Patriot, the National Memorial Day Parade is ENIRELY FUNDED by private contributions, including from generous patriots like you.** **[In light of America’s 250th birthday and the special opportunity we have to honor veterans this year, will you chip in today to help support the parade with a gift of $25, $50, $75, $150, or more?](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=59227a9cc4c34343b25228e6fdd3eea2&_e=qZkR5r4NLP0mGVx0Yv42entrTvtbOyqvo3ogmPKnJbCB0VdsVv-TWU990rm3YHDCbXXr4CpooZNsLzKUMwzbohLwlRUjHZpZhDbWppKaczCXBzxWsWSv-GlvhK0hLVx5ZE10MipE58dbiHEuLBmHLU6Q7jWje6cuS8Z9lHJBWX8mPn4OfRbcw4EACzCTWa6azwhJq-AXSILw9R9diUtmCJQMMgZ62J7_vNK3Y8rJpwyacCAaGp-wR3b38tBq-OzC_FJA1SfS7gd0zAoXldxFxDFxF485NhCjSy_WkYzN4V8UIj_GzCphXdYSYjrX904PY3trHe7rgIweFUhxc1Kb-i9lJA57HN39l9OxdZG8y1l-oXbTRCVnwrFgfPSGy09XMFZG0oOrMgaCeONuCI15ZkCnKhnXywZcaXZeR2E329C8m96yg8CYJ49Q1em0xUidrmxZ1KaaUcr5h7T4UhGgLOTzCTaOHxAbwtCYzBJKjEwk6oWomA445KYauwN6fDEjd-bqcPcXmVnm6KD5LBcbTeODWHJXbvpwAzpq2s1d84oGbXp81gSY5w-2uRZllFVURyOn0E8Wcv84WsI_YWuyMw1uyoFENvttQrvsm_SwQA2aRx_K5eWEv1b0eBtjcYcfciIWq1UAtMqHR9aga0wa66__ilt_WisASXuJPyMfo75aCs4kyQbPDI3TgVDtx7eT)**

Historically, AVC has been able to do this at a fraction of what other major public events cost to put on each year. In fact, _the entire parade_ costs about as much as just THREE balloons in the Macy's Thanksgiving Day Parade or TWO floats in the Rose Bowl Parade! _That means that every dollar that's contributed by a generous American like you makes a difference for this important event._ **But I want to reiterate that American Veterans Center does not take ANY federal funding for this event. It is funded ****_entirely_**** by private citizens like you who want to honor those who have sacrificed so much for our country.** **[Patriot, as we look ahead to this historic event, can I count on your generous gift of $25, $50, $75, $150, or more?](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=59227a9cc4c34343b25228e6fdd3eea2&_e=qZkR5r4NLP0mGVx0Yv42entrTvtbOyqvo3ogmPKnJbCB0VdsVv-TWU990rm3YHDCbXXr4CpooZNsLzKUMwzbohLwlRUjHZpZhDbWppKaczCXBzxWsWSv-GlvhK0hLVx5ZE10MipE58dbiHEuLBmHLU6Q7jWje6cuS8Z9lHJBWX8mPn4OfRbcw4EACzCTWa6azwhJq-AXSILw9R9diUtmCJQMMgZ62J7_vNK3Y8rJpwyacCAaGp-wR3b38tBq-OzCXK25dCADPBHCEaE2ynC6fAVsKm3EhYIFxTAV3pqbhPBqe7CKeDstvk9S3at-44757OxKTLvbHQ5gSmk0cvjgUk-UT-cbc2FBpnRDseHq9Ksjsj2A0rSf9M9f6WdG4DZSRubaAKSdXG9rd9ngc0l7PJZ9QCfNyLpEslqZC89F0C3284b1oA5ye-qm_ala7eJc1pUWba7G_8FZlivxj3EE4qscVLFDXLaayTkUjfz7khGSdA7gKpk7suB3dkTqVY2OuUQyUWPMDNFYDWob9J8PAkVKmk5TQD9nxEBJrK2YLNSV1_kejcfn1AdozYvb7ShsmIzOWvHnwKefDMmI5rSv4YYSONsJTfGiFnQUdVQQClPLSSeGCyFSjg3QEdDqco8NkKxfn38AcEANHF06sIzhYnrpsA2vSSLrGOmYhZH7xRChEtr2nuHrdNlAbTgDexOl)[](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=59227a9cc4c34343b25228e6fdd3eea2&_e=qZkR5r4NLP0mGVx0Yv42entrTvtbOyqvo3ogmPKnJbCB0VdsVv-TWU990rm3YHDCbXXr4CpooZNsLzKUMwzbohLwlRUjHZpZhDbWppKaczCXBzxWsWSv-GlvhK0hLVx5ZE10MipE58dbiHEuLBmHLU6Q7jWje6cuS8Z9lHJBWX8mPn4OfRbcw4EACzCTWa6azwhJq-AXSILw9R9diUtmCJQMMgZ62J7_vNK3Y8rJpwyacCAaGp-wR3b38tBq-OzC1n2tny7S5jMzDHUk0sruJUDQ5vwY9tfJNynUywzZb5JT8MYFTJDSP2in13GQsEsdG1aE8_0lU_EBeBjQbpysoK2c6zEOo5zHd0QVvx-H-jEMTDec7Z0nMTKYVoH69iO2oF86xuSD0uCR7kmgWoNTpccnyC7Tbi3YmrrnQtaG0TezseQhVDDgrEbZ22O6p2NPmshW1hBDflFOsP8feEqmkas-i2NpGmXsL2ROF7QnzwxQOyWc80bSMNDjpZdBfA3E9rNDHW1phqRHYAvfBMgFzpQYNJt_Kq-xWEZU3vnC_oS1FE05UOpTcnx4HRKg2b5_uU-rTiBOPMfezFPUi3eiL5tpfmkOn3c1nWE1Fuv2rQmx5iV9RL8NOU-YSXDakyH_zPR_jpJbH0VpTxOmj93jER4mX5CgXklXK_DFezF-BEr1ZxOcHH8M5UIQ-ZxzYR4M)**

| | Your support of the American Veterans Center not only helps the parade, but also supports great programs like:
---
* Record hundreds of oral histories of veterans from World War II to today before we lose these stories forever.

* Hold our annual American Veterans Conference, which allows young cadets a chance to hear first-hand from veterans from WWII, Korea, Vietnam, and our modern wars.

* Produce veteran-focused T.V. documentaries and specials that remind Americans the price of our freedom.

---

On Memorial Day - not just this momentous year of military milestones, but every year - please join me in honoring those who made the ultimate sacrifice for our freedoms. Come to the National Memorial Day Parade in Washington, D.C. Visit a local veterans' cemetery. Send a note to a family of the fallen. Or simply take a quiet moment to think about the men and women who gave their lives to defend OUR country. I believe that while we can never do enough to express our gratitude to our defenders, veterans, and first responders, we can always do a little more. **Patriot, thank you for your support of America 's heroes. ** [**And please, if you’re able, make a gift to fund the National Memorial Day parade right now during America’s 250****th****birthday.**](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=59227a9cc4c34343b25228e6fdd3eea2&_e=qZkR5r4NLP0mGVx0Yv42entrTvtbOyqvo3ogmPKnJbCB0VdsVv-TWU990rm3YHDCbXXr4CpooZNsLzKUMwzbohLwlRUjHZpZhDbWppKaczCXBzxWsWSv-GlvhK0hLVx5ZE10MipE58dbiHEuLBmHLU6Q7jWje6cuS8Z9lHJBWX8mPn4OfRbcw4EACzCTWa6azwhJq-AXSILw9R9diUtmCJQMMgZ62J7_vNK3Y8rJpwyacCAaGp-wR3b38tBq-OzCGJoIr8dYxnZFu_aXUcHNJSuJuwvgRpDaS3j8IsnJqCjA0rT78Lvb5mj30Yrfsv8_yU2vvnafJfPfOlqKlR14ODIQmuchkfpt0JslTxOyDgnavDJ-4PKMt5PL4bSYT7ZvKxGtEuc8q3ojDQ0Jr0XKu1__tn9oD09QEAP9nfUJqYXLvvbq7_RpEhTZRofU1yTSaMVIJj51zr7JLidfmIb5DOG2N1SDXERjNxpZO8-KdqUSw2rZjwQfCcer6zh_o1zo7cojlrwT_2aild1awA2c68SHRxOxuLc7gO97ABfqyvYFVkuTGnmvW3VgHdFnU2Id7KQgh2kUf5lNRG6fepWjEU7HX06-fn-Mmt_EUc3D8RKIgG7AmjjGmvPdY4X_7_rH9MVhOCX4JPH2BbPfitK97c_UNLD4lYwPpaLIo9UWafY7AqCujfv0hEiRGeTb47IC)

clean_body: The biggest National Memorial Day Parade yet | | | Patriot, It's Gary Sinise here. You may know me from my roles in movies like Apollo 13 and The Green Mile or the CBS television series CSI: NY - but it was in 1994 playing the role of Lt. Dan in Forrest Gump that changed my life and inspired my enduring connection to our defenders, veterans, and first responders. I've served as an Honorary Grand Marshal for the National Memorial Day Parade in Washington, D.C. nearly every year since American Veterans Center revived it in 2005. But I've never been more excited to serve as an Honorary Grand Marshall than I am this year - the year of America's 250th birthday. Patriot, this Memorial Day marks the first major national act of remembrance of the veterans who made the ultimate sacrifice to defend America from its enemies and ensure it has remained the Land of the Free for the last 250 years. And this year's National Memorial Day Parade will be a special edition - the biggest parade yet - where national memory meets living history as veterans from every military generation celebrate America and honor the service and sacrifice of their fallen brethren. Patriot, the National Memorial Day Parade is ENIRELY FUNDED by private contributions, including from generous patriots like you. The 2026 National Memorial Day Parade is a once-in-a-lifetime moment where we need to do all we can to honor our country and our veterans. Bigger crowds. More veterans. More young people learning what courage, service, and sacrifice really mean. Historically, AVC has been able to do this at a fraction of what other major public events cost to put on each year. In fact, the entire parade costs about as much as just THREE balloons in the Macy's Thanksgiving Day Parade or TWO floats in the Rose Bowl Parade! That means that every dollar that's contributed by a generous American like you makes a difference for this important event. But I want to reiterate that American Veterans Center does not take ANY federal funding for this event. It is funded entirely by private citizens like you who want to honor those who have sacrificed so much for our country. | | Your support of the American Veterans Center not only helps the parade, but also supports great programs like: Record hundreds of oral histories of veterans from World War II to today before we lose these stories forever. Hold our annual American Veterans Conference, which allows young cadets a chance to hear first-hand from veterans from WWII, Korea, Vietnam, and our modern wars. Produce veteran-focused T.V. documentaries and specials that remind Americans the price of our freedom. But of course, the National Memorial Day Parade is AVC's flagship event honoring America's veterans. And this year's parade will be the largest in their history of hosting the parade. If you've been looking for an opportunity to give back to those who have given so much for our freedom, this is one of the best possible ways you could do it. And for a special group of veterans, this might be one of the last chances we get. You see, with each year that goes by, we lose more and more of our World War II veterans, and for the few we have remaining, traveling to Washington, D.C., for the parade is difficult. | | | | | WWII veterans were a key part of the first parade when it was revived in 2005, as they have been every year since for the last 20 years. But this may be the last year we have the opportunity to have these brave men and women with us for the parade. This year is a rare moment when living witnesses to World War II and America 's 250**th birthday overlap. And no matter how much you can give to this important cause, I want to ask something additional of you. On Memorial Day - not just this momentous year of military milestones, but every year - please join me in honoring those who made the ultimate sacrifice for our freedoms. Come to the National Memorial Day Parade in Washington, D.C. Visit a local veterans' cemetery. Send a note to a family of the fallen. Or simply take a quiet moment to think about the men and women who gave their lives to defend OUR country. I believe that while we can never do enough to express our gratitude to our defenders, veterans, and first responders, we can always do a little more. Patriot, thank you for your support of America 's heroes. Sincerely, Gary Sinise Honarary Grand MarshalNational Memorial Day Parade P.S. Patriot, America is stepping into its 250th year, and this Memorial Day begins that national remembrance. Veterans across generations have given their lives to defend America and all she stands for. And for the Greatest Generation in particular, this may be the last time they're able to join us at this year's National Memorial Day Parade. There's no better send-off we can give them than to make sure they're present to honor their fallen brethren during America's 250th birthday. The mission of the American Veterans Center and the World War II Veterans Committee is to preserve and promote the legacy and experiences of America's veterans and active duty service personnel from World War II through today. A non-profit educational organization, the AVC and WWIIVC are funded solely through generous contributions from people like you. All contributions are tax-deductible. # **Can your family pass a basic civics test?**
---
| | [](https://email.updates.conservativeinnovation.com/c/eJyUkM2KpDAUhZ8m7kryYxJdZDFMIQxU1WI2s5Q7yVUDMZEkWvTbNzb9Ar27fHz3cDj_ntMTfPBxecGGxp5-ymV6-8i0Vi6FALlUBLv6uHykY-KUqxvtbqy_PN44Y50SvEFzPUjWSdE3q3GKU82FdFYqkGy2g7Iau_-z1lSyeWi8uZJox3rGmRS61coKKtXcU8bUrBnp6LE7qFham2LBfEL1J_oY03Wl2Nq0NcGste6FiF-Ej4SPGBdYsN0zLJiPSyF8rInwcf37eIYwPogYj7pNJR3ZIhF3QMLVRTZ0_tiIuO-Qa8T8jS1sO_glEnH__ec1DGxgTTZuf_sQfFGadHTZwIevOvXnA56GfwYAAP__c5d9TQ)

Most Americans can't answer basic questions about how our government works, its history, or why our freedoms matter. Sadly, this is true for many adults--but it's especially true for younger generations. If we don't teach young people what makes America unique and help them understand why freedom is important, we risk losing the liberty America's Founders secured for us. **[Take the Family Civics Sweepstakes Challenge and test your family's knowledge!](https://email.updates.conservativeinnovation.com/c/eJyUkM2KpDAUhZ8m7kryYxJdZDFMIQxU1WI2s5Q7yVUDMZEkWvTbNzb9Ar27fHz3cDj_ntMTfPBxecGGxp5-ymV6-8i0Vi6FALlUBLv6uHykY-KUqxvtbqy_PN44Y50SvEFzPUjWSdE3q3GKU82FdFYqkGy2g7Iau_-z1lSyeWi8uZJox3rGmRS61coKKtXcU8bUrBnp6LE7qFham2LBfEL1J_oY03Wl2Nq0NcGste6FiF-Ej4SPGBdYsN0zLJiPSyF8rInwcf37eIYwPogYj7pNJR3ZIhF3QMLVRTZ0_tiIuO-Qa8T8jS1sO_glEnH__ec1DGxgTTZuf_sQfFGadHTZwIevOvXnA56GfwYAAP__c5d9TQ)**

These kid-friendly lessons make it easy to learn together, laugh together, and have meaningful conversations about what makes our country great. As a family, answer 26 questions about American civics, history, and government, and you could be one of 13 families who win! (Sweepstakes ends on May 25, 2026)

name: Virginians For Fair Elections
subject: Super Saturday is here!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Virginians for Fair Elections
body:
# [](https://act.virginiansforfairelections.com/go/629?refcode=em20260418-532&t=1&akid=532%2E12592%2EPuC45k)
**Today is Super Saturday.**
Right now, hundreds of volunteers are spreading across every corner of Virginia, knocking on doors, dropping literature at people’s doorsteps, and talking to real voters on the final day of early voting.
We have one job today: **reach every undecided voter we can before Election Day on April 21st.**
But we need your help to keep our volunteers going.
[](https://act.virginiansforfairelections.com/go/41?c0=em_ms_vafe_20260418_1.g7s8N1&amount=3&refcode=20260418-532&t=2&akid=532%2E12592%2EPuC45k)
We’re 90% of the way to meeting our $40,000 today to fully fund our ground operation. Every dollar goes directly to keeping our volunteers in the field: _supplies, gas, and the infrastructure to reach thousands of doors._
📋 $10 puts a clipboard and literature packet in a volunteer's hands right now.
⛽ $50 covers gas for an organizer driving across a region.
✅ $75 fuels an entire canvassing team for the day.
The other side isn't slowing down. Republicans are spending and organizing with their millions in MAGA dark money. That’s because Trump and his MAGA allies know exactly what's at stake.
[**Can you chip in $3 to our Super Saturday Fund right now and keep our ground game running?**](https://act.virginiansforfairelections.com/go/41?c0=em_ms_vafe_20260418_1.g7s8N1&amount=3&refcode=20260418-532&t=3&akid=532%2E12592%2EPuC45k)
**[Donate $3 ››](https://act.virginiansforfairelections.com/go/41?c0=em_ms_vafe_20260418_1.g7s8N1&amount=3&refcode=20260418-532&t=22&akid=532%2E12592%2EPuC45k)**
Thank you for your support,
Virginians for Fair Elections

[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_vafe_2026_footer?refcode=em&t=23&refcode2=532_12592_PuC45k&akid=532%2E12592%2EPuC45k)
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, so thank you for reading to the end.
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://vafe.actionkit.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=24&akid=532%2E12592%2EPuC45k)
* [Update My Personal Information](https://vafe.actionkit.com/signup/update-contact-information/?&t=25&akid=532%2E12592%2EPuC45k)
* [Unsubscribe](https://vafe.actionkit.com/signup/unsubscribe/?t=26&akid=532%2E12592%2EPuC45k)

Contributions or gifts to Virginians for Fair Elections are not tax deductible.

name: Douglas Carswell
subject: Mississippi moves up rankings — and it is not an accident!
body:
| | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/mspolicy/msmomentum-12852964?e=5703353baa)
---
| |
---
| _**If you're not in front of our Saturday email, you're behind.**_
---
| Dear Friend,
The latest _Rich States, Poor States_ index has just been published by ALEC, and for anyone who cares about the direction of our state, it makes for encouraging reading.

**Mississippi now ranks 24th in America for economic outlook.** That is a forward-looking measure, built from fifteen different state policy variables — tax burden, regulatory environment, labor law, and more. A decade ago, the idea of Mississippi sitting in the top half of that table would have been difficult to imagine. Today, we are there — and we are still climbing.

**Here are three charts to brighten your Saturday morning!**

The first is about the size of government. The number of public sector employees in Mississippi is falling — which means the private sector, which is what actually generates wealth, is becoming more prominent.
---
|
---
| The second chart tells a story about the cost of doing business in our state. The non-wage costs of hiring people in our state have fallen steadily — one of the reasons so many firms are now choosing to invest here rather than somewhere else.
---
|
---
| The third is about people — and it may be the most important of all. For too long, young Mississippians have moved to Birmingham, Nashville, or Austin to find better opportunities. Now, at last, the trend is reversing. **More people are moving to Mississippi than leaving it.**
---
|
---
| In 2021, Mississippi enacted serious labor market reform — making it easier for people to work, to train, and to move between careers. In 2022, we replaced an old, progressive tax code with a flat tax. Year after year, we have kept our energy among the most affordable in the country — a quiet advantage that every family and every employer benefits from. In 2024, we passed education funding reform so that the money at last follows the child. In 2025, we enacted the historic elimination of the state income tax itself — a policy that only a few years ago was dismissed as politically impossible. And this year, in 2026, we have begun to take on the thicket of red tape that has held back our healthcare economy.

Every one of these reforms was resisted. Everyone was said, at the time, to be too ambitious or too politically risky. Everyone now stands as part of the answer to why Mississippi is moving up the rankings.
Ideas do not turn into law without people willing to fight to make it happen. **Sycophancy might get you into the signing ceremony. It takes robust advocacy to ensure that there’s a bill to be signed in the first place.**

The rest of the country is beginning to notice. **Mississippi is no longer the state that others use as a punchline. We are becoming a state that others are studying.**

There is a great deal still to do. But the direction of travel is clear — and you have helped set it.

Have a wonderful weekend!
---
| [Forward this email to a friend!](https://mspolicy.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=60d4052c66b6745831ccdf7c8&id=cfdd192db6&e=5703353baa "Forward this email to a friend!")
---
| |
---
Warm regards,

Douglas Carswell
President & CEO
---
|
---

# [Children First - Close the Loopholes](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1454853&post_id=194562287&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTk0NTYyMjg3LCJpYXQiOjE3NzY1MTY1MDYsImV4cCI6MTc3OTEwODUwNiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE0NTQ4NTMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.evlXYHBQ6tI5BrDQCRIW2pmHEWin_vpglfjS9D-yxIU)
### If We Won't Stand Up for the Children, What Are We Governing For?
| | [Jonathan Goldstein](https://substack.com/@jonathangoldstein81) and [Dr. Michael Goldstein](https://substack.com/@drmichaelgoldsteinct)
---
| Apr 18
---
| [](https://substack.com/@jonathangoldstein81)| | [](https://substack.com/@drmichaelgoldsteinct)
---|---|---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1454853&post_id=194562287&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTk0NTYyMjg3LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc3NjUxNjUwNiwiZXhwIjoxNzc5MTA4NTA2LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMTQ1NDg1MyIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.H-m1YQhMK3v3Mv2wp1URaht7swuInsRNbeubVVTOJEQ&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/20f57457-e3ca-41f4-b66a-c675a596e839?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|

[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1454853&post_id=194562287&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTk0NTYyMjg3LCJpYXQiOjE3NzY1MTY1MDYsImV4cCI6MTc3OTEwODUwNiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE0NTQ4NTMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.evlXYHBQ6tI5BrDQCRIW2pmHEWin_vpglfjS9D-yxIU)
**With so many bills emerging from this short session, and many of them raising serious questions, it is important to call out the ones that are genuinely good.**

Not every bill deserves praise simply because it is filed. But when legislation directly addresses a real danger, closes an obvious loophole, and puts the safety of children first, it should be recognized for what it is: responsible lawmaking.

**Child safety is not optional. It is not partisan. It is one of the most basic duties of government.
**
_Good legislators do not wait for the next scandal, the next headline, or the next child to be put at risk. They act proactively where they can, and when serious gaps are exposed, they respond with legislation designed to prevent and deter future harm to Connecticut children and the families they represent.
_
That is why [Rep. Tracy Marra's](https://substack.com/redirect/b09f0e1d-c7e3-434c-975a-0301c12291ee?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) role here matters.

Marra, who represents Darien and Norwalk, introduced **[HB 5327](https://substack.com/redirect/06321ed1-3545-4a49-abc3-fc9f523d0006?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) **through the Education Committee in direct response to a serious gap in school hiring transparency that she and her constituents experienced in Darien. Instead of a press release or vigil, she took action.
At a time when plenty of proposals coming out of Hartford deserve skepticism, this is the kind of bill that deserves support. It addresses a real problem, responds to substantiated facts on the ground, and focuses on protecting children rather than scoring political points.

_**The Darien case made that gap impossible to ignore.**_

Reporting on former Darien High School teacher Sean Boardman revealed that prior issues tied to his employment in Stratford and East Haddam were not fully disclosed during the hiring process. The ability for an employee to move between districts without meaningful transparency (or "**shuffling** ") exposed a structural weakness, one that placed children at risk and left school systems without the information they needed to make informed decisions. As a mother herself, Marra stated in her testimony before the Education Committee _" we need to be the adults in the room and we need to protect our kids"_
_**But Darien is not an isolated incident.**_

In Stamford, a lawsuit alleges that school officials failed to stop abuse by former elementary school teacher Andrew Park. According to reporting, Park was arrested in April 2024 and later faced additional allegations, with claims that warning signs were missed or ignored, allowing abuse to continue.

Together, the Darien and Stamford cases reveal the same underlying problem: _**systems that rely on incomplete disclosures, quiet resignations, and institutional silence are not sufficient to protect children**_. When one case exposes a hiring loophole and another raises questions about internal accountability, the conclusion is unavoidable: stronger safeguards are necessary.

That is exactly what HB 5327 attempts to do.
The bill's title leaves no ambiguity:

**" AN ACT AUTHORIZING THE DISCLOSURE OF AN OPEN INVESTIGATION OF ABUSE OR NEGLECT OR SEXUAL MISCONDUCT IN THE HIRING PROCESS OF SCHOOL EMPLOYEES."**

The proposal expands disclosure requirements well beyond narrow existing categories. It requires applicants to affirmatively disclose whether they:

_**" have been or are the subject of an abuse or neglect or sexual misconduct investigation, or any investigation involving the injury or risk of injury to, or impairing the morals of, a minor"
**_
This is not technical language. It is a necessary safeguard. It closes the door on ambiguity and prevents applicants from avoiding disclosure through narrow interpretations of employment questions that are usually Yes or No.

The bill also directly addresses the common tactic of resigning during an investigation. It requires disclosure of whether an applicant:

_**" resigned from or otherwise separated from any employment while an allegation of abuse or neglect was pending or under investigation ... or an allegation involving the injury or risk of injury to, or impairing the morals of, a minor was pending or under investigation"
**_
It further requires prior employers to disclose whether an applicant:

_**" was disciplined or asked to resign from employment or resigned from or otherwise separated from any employment while an allegation ... was or is pending or under investigation"**_

Just as importantly, the bill prohibits "**secrecy arrangements "** to suppress critical information. No school authority (including private schools) may enter into an agreement that:

**" has the effect of suppressing or requiring the nondisclosure of information relating to a pending investigation"
**
And it includes one of the strongest protections in the proposal: a school district may not hire an applicant who is currently under investigation for abuse, neglect, or misconduct involving a minor.

This is what responsible lawmaking looks like.

In a session where many bills have been questionable, this is one of the measures worth calling out as a good bill. It is specific. It is practical. It responds to a known risk. And it puts the focus exactly where it belongs: on protecting children.

_Unfortunately, Darien exposed the loophole. Rep. Marra has publicly said the bill was developed in response to a difficult situation in her district and was intended to better protect schoolchildren by requiring disclosure of open or pending investigations during the school hiring process.
_
Stamford reinforced the stakes that bad actors in education are not an isolated incident in our school systems.

Good legislators do not ignore those warning signs. They act to close the gaps before more harm occurs as this type of harm to our children may be irreversible.

And that brings the broader issue into focus.

If the state is serious about protecting children, institutional safety must come first. The public entrusts schools with daily custody, supervision, and authority over children. That responsibility demands transparency, accountability, and rigorous hiring safeguards.

This is what citizens expect. It is not selective protection.

Only after those institutional vulnerabilities are addressed should isolated incidents of child neglect in homeschooling be elevated as a primary issue for elected officials. Until then, Hartford should begin where its responsibility is greatest: ensuring that the institutions already entrusted with children are as safe, transparent, and accountable as possible.
## **Balance Within our Judicial Framework**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/2a1413bc-3beb-4009-86e0-79d69e70fff5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
The language will be debated within the confines of our system of justice. A good result will be with respect to focusing on those "**substantiated investigations "** and "**convictions "** in the arena of child safety while _**ruling out false allegations.**_
_**This cannot be a bill that prevents any applicant who has been targeted with frivolous allegations (as anyone can file a suit). This is still a justice system that presumes innocence until proven guilty; this must be balanced to ensure verification of substantiation of a claim coupled with a 360-degree evaluation of the character of the applicant.**_
_**Even one false allegation can destroy a teacher 's life -- but the answer is not to ignore claims. It is to investigate quickly, confidentially, and fairly so children are protected and innocent teachers are not publicly executed before facts are known.

**_Good legislators protect children by closing real loopholes.

_**If we won 't protect kids, what are we governing for?**_

[https://www.stamfordadvocate.com/local/article/stamford-teacher-sex-assault-lawsuit-andrew-park-22159163.php](https://substack.com/redirect/28631b2e-a6c2-4697-b56f-dd3b9cee5026?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_

That is exactly what HB 5327 attempts to do. The bill's title leaves no ambiguity: " AN ACT AUTHORIZING THE DISCLOSURE OF AN OPEN INVESTIGATION OF ABUSE OR NEGLECT OR SEXUAL MISCONDUCT IN THE HIRING PROCESS OF SCHOOL EMPLOYEES." The proposal expands disclosure requirements well beyond narrow existing categories. It requires applicants to affirmatively disclose whether they: " have been or are the subject of an abuse or neglect or sexual misconduct investigation, or any investigation involving the injury or risk of injury to, or impairing the morals of, a minor" This is not technical language. It is a necessary safeguard. It closes the door on ambiguity and prevents applicants from avoiding disclosure through narrow interpretations of employment questions that are usually Yes or No. The bill also directly addresses the common tactic of resigning during an investigation. It requires disclosure of whether an applicant: " resigned from or otherwise separated from any employment while an allegation of abuse or neglect was pending or under investigation ... or an allegation involving the injury or risk of injury to, or impairing the morals of, a minor was pending or under investigation" It further requires prior employers to disclose whether an applicant: " was disciplined or asked to resign from employment or resigned from or otherwise separated from any employment while an allegation ... was or is pending or under investigation" Just as importantly, the bill prohibits "secrecy arrangements " to suppress critical information. No school authority (including private schools) may enter into an agreement that: " has the effect of suppressing or requiring the nondisclosure of information relating to a pending investigation" And it includes one of the strongest protections in the proposal: a school district may not hire an applicant who is currently under investigation for abuse, neglect, or misconduct involving a minor. This is what responsible lawmaking looks like. In a session where many bills have been questionable, this is one of the measures worth calling out as a good bill. It is specific. It is practical. It responds to a known risk. And it puts the focus exactly where it belongs: on protecting children. Unfortunately, Darien exposed the loophole. Rep. Marra has publicly said the bill was developed in response to a difficult situation in her district and was intended to better protect schoolchildren by requiring disclosure of open or pending investigations during the school hiring process. Stamford reinforced the stakes that bad actors in education are not an isolated incident in our school systems. Good legislators do not ignore those warning signs. They act to close the gaps before more harm occurs as this type of harm to our children may be irreversible. And that brings the broader issue into focus. If the state is serious about protecting children, institutional safety must come first. The public entrusts schools with daily custody, supervision, and authority over children. That responsibility demands transparency, accountability, and rigorous hiring safeguards. This is what citizens expect. It is not selective protection. Only after those institutional vulnerabilities are addressed should isolated incidents of child neglect in homeschooling be elevated as a primary issue for elected officials. Until then, Hartford should begin where its responsibility is greatest: ensuring that the institutions already entrusted with children are as safe, transparent, and accountable as possible. Balance Within our Judicial Framework | | ---|---|--- The language will be debated within the confines of our system of justice. A good result will be with respect to focusing on those "substantiated investigations " and "convictions " in the arena of child safety while ruling out false allegations. This cannot be a bill that prevents any applicant who has been targeted with frivolous allegations (as anyone can file a suit). This is still a justice system that presumes innocence until proven guilty; this must be balanced to ensure verification of substantiation of a claim coupled with a 360-degree evaluation of the character of the applicant. Even one false allegation can destroy a teacher 's life -- but the answer is not to ignore claims. It is to investigate quickly, confidentially, and fairly so children are protected and innocent teachers are not publicly executed before facts are known. Good legislators protect children by closing real loopholes. If we won 't protect kids, what are we governing for? Sources ** | A guest post by| Consultant, Political Consultant, Campaign Manager, Construction Consultant and Corporate Attorney. Jonathan served as the Campaign Manager for Dr. Michael Goldstein - CT-04 The Goldstein Substack is free today. But if you enjoyed this post, you can tell The Goldstein Substack that their writing is valuable by pledging a future subscription. You won't be charged unless they enable payments. | | | | | | | (C) 2026 Michael Goldstein ? ?

name: Daniel Cameron
subject: Grateful for my amazing wife and our new little one
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cameron for Senate, Inc
body:
| | | | | |
---
| | [](https://eot.cameronforkentucky.com/f/a/JaU_7i1JOo7mjpSGR6FA6g~~/AAAHURA~/x3XAdo-eGOkA9knV5y1FimOnzyi2kMlX0zyveUsYHEc1tUNCwzq-jayj3lLQ5504YYiiOEQo2sP1HGwfpy6i8HTWFiPgkbYahK-p_z7MR7QRh1s75N_w1YbjuABm6v8duFLL7Qka20fs4vuJFOpjOa8UthCv5zv34CNZGxzjsed3tmzu70Mri2tYUUBkAXpPOzWSBBt7UbgiBSmofOSStpw0ZqpBOqdI1BZdm0uNGc6d9T6hOHnxTR67QUvVqNZfwWaH6TWoPhbcikf8H2UHRA~~)

Friend,
I wanted to take a moment to share how truly thankful I am for my beautiful wife, Makenze.
With our six-week-old son Harrison in tow, she came with me on the road to speak on behalf of Team Cameron. Watching her stand up there with Harrison strapped to her chest, speaking on our shared values and vision for America filled my heart with pride.
Makenze is not only an incredible partner and mother to our boys, but she’s also been a steadfast source of strength throughout this journey. **Her example reminds me every single day why we’re fighting so hard: ****to protect and strengthen American families just like ours.**
**Strong families are the foundation of a strong nation.** That’s why Makenze and I are committed to policies that support working parents, lower the cost of living, and honor the values that make America great.
[_**If you believe in the power of strong families as much as we do, would you consider supporting us today?**_](https://eot.cameronforkentucky.com/f/a/JaU_7i1JOo7mjpSGR6FA6g~~/AAAHURA~/x3XAdo-eGOkA9knV5y1FimOnzyi2kMlX0zyveUsYHEc1tUNCwzq-jayj3lLQ5504YYiiOEQo2sP1HGwfpy6i8HTWFiPgkbYahK-p_z7MR7QRh1s75N_w1YbjuABm6v8duFLL7Qka20fs4vuJFOpjOa8UthCv5zv34CNZGxzjsed3tmzu70Mri2tYUUBkAXpPOzWSBBt7UbgiBSmofOSStpw0ZqpBOqdI1BZdm0uNGc6d9T6hOHnxTR67QUvVqNZfwWaH6TWoPhbcikf8H2UHRA~~)

name: Roy Cooper
subject: Will you add your name to personally endorse my campaign?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cooper Victory Fund and authorized by Cooper for North Carolina
body:
I wanted to reach out to you specifically, Peter.
|
# [](https://act.roycooper.com/go/6532?source=&t=1&akid=1090%2E124072%2ENkofHd)
| | | |
---
[](https://act.roycooper.com/survey/endorse-cooper-2026-email-v2?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&ask=5&source=jtk282-su-nd&t=8&akid=1090%2E124072%2ENkofHd)
Peter,

I’m Roy Cooper, and I’m the Democratic nominee for U.S. Senate in North Carolina. Today, I wanted to reach out to you specifically not to ask for a contribution – but instead, to share a bit more about myself and ask for your personal endorsement in this race.
**[If you’re already all in to flip North Carolina blue and take back the Senate, please add your name now to help us build the grassroots movement we need to win.](https://act.roycooper.com/survey/endorse-cooper-2026-email-v2?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&ask=5&source=jtk282-su-nd&t=9&akid=1090%2E124072%2ENkofHd)**
Otherwise, let me explain why your support, and your signature today, is so important.

I’ve spent my life serving the people of North Carolina.
As Governor, I expanded Medicaid to hundreds of thousands of hardworking people and fought for better health care. As Attorney General, I protected working families from fraud and abuse. As a State Legislator, I pushed for policies that made everyday people’s lives easier.
**But before all of this, I was just a boy from Nash County – the son of a public school teacher who tried to do right by his family, friends and neighbors.**
Now, I’m running for the U.S. Senate because I’ve seen what’s possible when government actually puts people first. When those in power lead with a sense of duty, purpose and moral clarity. That’s not happening in Washington DC right now, and we have to change that.
**[The path to retaking the Senate majority for Democrats runs straight through North Carolina – and with a recent poll showing this race statistically tied, we can win but not without a powerful grassroots team behind me. So please, will you add your name to personally endorse my campaign?](https://act.roycooper.com/survey/endorse-cooper-2026-email-v2?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&ask=5&source=jtk282-su-nd&t=10&akid=1090%2E124072%2ENkofHd)**
| | [ADD YOUR NAME](https://act.roycooper.com/survey/endorse-cooper-2026-email-v2?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&ask=5&source=jtk282-su-nd&t=11&akid=1090%2E124072%2ENkofHd)
---
Every signature is an act of faith in the movement we’re building together. Thank you.

Roy Cooper
| |
* * *
[](https://act.roycooper.com/go/8188?t=2&akid=1090%2E124072%2ENkofHd)
Democrat Roy Cooper is running for U.S. Senate to help take back the Majority for Democrats. North Carolina is Democrats’ best chance to flip a seat from red to blue — the path to stopping Republicans in Washington starts here.

National Republicans will target North Carolina as a key battleground and invest heavily in defeating us.

Cooper is a former state legislator, Attorney General, and most recently Governor of North Carolina. North Carolina will be the most competitive Senate election of 2026, and Cooper enters the race as the most formidable candidate of either party.

Cooper’s signature achievements include helping to expand Medicaid and helping make NC the top state for business by creating thousands of jobs and growing the clean energy economy.

**_[Donate today to help win this seat and flip the Senate blue.](https://act.roycooper.com/go/6533?source=&t=3&akid=1090%2E124072%2ENkofHd)_**
| | [DONATE](https://act.roycooper.com/go/6297?source=&t=4&akid=1090%2E124072%2ENkofHd)
---
| | [DONATE MONTHLY](https://act.roycooper.com/go/6298?source=&t=5&akid=1090%2E124072%2ENkofHd)
---
| | [SHOP OUR MERCH STORE](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2133?t=6&akid=1090%2E124072%2ENkofHd)
---
[info@roycooper.com](mailto:info@roycooper.com) | [Privacy Policy](https://act.roycooper.com/go/825?t=7&akid=1090%2E124072%2ENkofHd)
Cooper Victory Fund
PO Box 1190
Raleigh, NC 27602
Paid for by Cooper Victory Fund and authorized by Cooper for North Carolina.

The first $3,500 of a person’s contribution and the first $5,000 of a PAC’s contribution will be designated for the primary election; the next $3,500/$5,000 will be designated for the general election. The next $10,000 from a person and $5,000 from a PAC will be allocated to North Carolina Democratic Party – Federal. The next $44,300 of a contribution from a person and the next $15,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC General Fund. name: BPR Daily AM

---
Hi Peter,

I wanted to make sure you saw this message – because I can tell it left an impression on many of you when we sent it earlier this week.

Last weekend, JD Vance attempted high-stakes peace talks aimed at ending the war...and we have yet to reach any kind of deal. Experts are now warning that we are at serious risk of a global recession as this war continues.

There were 0 women with any kind of decision-making authority present for these negotiations – despite the fact that war can be especially devastating for women and children. It’s also worth mentioning that international data show that women’s involvement in global diplomacy leads to peace agreements that last 15+ years longer. **We have never been more sure that the work we do at Her Bold Move is of existential importance.**

In drastic times, we need drastic change. We believe that means MANY MORE women in the rooms where decisions about, literally, _world peace_ are being made.

**Women rarely have a seat at the table, as men across the world are failing to achieve peace. Her Bold Move is on the front lines of the effort to change that:** [**Help support Her Bold Move’s phenomenal roster of WOMEN candidates running for offices up and down the ballot and nationwide by chipping in $20.26 or more today. →**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/129697102/615250047/1701071684?refcode=EM_F_260418Peace_N_JZ_90DO_5_NG_N_L1&amount=20.26&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg4NDk1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjE2NDhhM2ExLTI4M2ItZjExMS04ZWYyLTAwMGQzYTE0YjY0MCIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=xc3d9ixMWkqC1sCCpKB2_42Ze01QHI0uCViXFBpuNzk=)

Together,

Jordan Zaslow
Founder + Executive Director
Her Bold Move

\---------- Forwarded message ---------
---
Hi Peter,

Over the weekend, JD Vance was sent to the Pakistani capital for high-stakes peace talks aimed at ending the war in Iran. The talks ended without any resolution.

Here’s the list of decision makers who were directly or indirectly involved in these talks:

**Donald Trump — United States
JD Vance — United States
Steve Witkoff – United States
Jared Kushner – United States
Mohammad Baqer Qalibaf — Iran
Abbas Araqchi — Iran
Shehbaz Sharif — Pakistan
Asim Munir — Pakistan
Ishaq Dar — Pakistan
Benjamin Netanyahu — Israel
Israel Katz — Israel**

Since the consequences of war can be especially devastating for women and children, we double-checked to see if there were any women with top-level decision making authority involved in these negotiations. The answer can be best illustrated in the images below:

(Photo by Jacquelyn Martin - Pool/Getty Images)
---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/129697104/615250049/1701071684?refcode=EM_F_260418Peace_N_JZ_90DO_5_NG_N_P2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg4NDk1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjE2NDhhM2ExLTI4M2ItZjExMS04ZWYyLTAwMGQzYTE0YjY0MCIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=xc3d9ixMWkqC1sCCpKB2_42Ze01QHI0uCViXFBpuNzk=)

They were welcomed by Pakistan’s army chief, Asim Munir, left; and Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar, right, in Islamabad, Pakistan, on Friday.Credit...Pakistan's Ministry of Foreign Affairs, via Reuters This is the way it is, but it’s not the way it has to be. Many of you are writing to us expressing fear that we are living in drastically troubling times. In drastic times, we need drastic change: Starting with having MANY MORE women in the rooms where decisions about, literally, _world peace_ are being made.

**According to data collected from international research between 1989-2011, when _women_ are involved in peace agreements, peace is 35% more likely to last 15+ years.**

[**When there are more women in government, the government works differently. Women rarely have a seat at the table as men across the world are failing to achieve peace. To solve this problem, Her Bold Move is working tirelessly to support incredible women who, we know, have what it takes to make progress in the rooms that matter. For starters, most of our candidates have a different profile than the typical politician: our candidates are social workers, immigration lawyers, public defenders, nurses, OB/GYNs, ministers, pastors, teachers, child advocates, domestic violence victim advocates, and more....

Call us naive, but we still believe the ultimate goal for any government should be world peace. The data show us that we NEED more women in government before we can even begin to imagine that possibility. **It is critical to our pursuit of de-escalating tensions and getting as close to world peace as possible.**

clean_body: | | | | | = Hi Peter, I wanted to make sure you saw this message – because I can tell it left an impression on many of you when we sent it earlier this week. Last weekend, JD Vance attempted high-stakes peace talks aimed at ending the war...and we have yet to reach any kind of deal. Experts are now warning that we are at serious risk of a global recession as this war continues. There were 0 women with any kind of decision-making authority present for these negotiations – despite the fact that war can be especially devastating for women and children. It’s also worth mentioning that international data show that women’s involvement in global diplomacy leads to peace agreements that last 15+ years longer. We have never been more sure that the work we do at Her Bold Move is of existential importance. In drastic times, we need drastic change. We believe that means MANY MORE women in the rooms where decisions about, literally, world peace are being made. Women rarely have a seat at the table, as men across the world are failing to achieve peace. Her Bold Move is on the front lines of the effort to change that: = Together, Jordan Zaslow Founder + Executive Director Her Bold Move \---------- Forwarded message --------- Hi Peter, Over the weekend, JD Vance was sent to the Pakistani capital for high-stakes peace talks aimed at ending the war in Iran. The talks ended without any resolution. Here’s the list of decision makers who were directly or indirectly involved in these talks: Donald Trump — United States JD Vance — United States Steve Witkoff – United States Jared Kushner – United States Mohammad Baqer Qalibaf — Iran Abbas Araqchi — Iran Shehbaz Sharif — Pakistan Asim Munir — Pakistan Ishaq Dar — Pakistan Benjamin Netanyahu — Israel Israel Katz — Israel Since the consequences of war can be especially devastating for women and children, we double-checked to see if there were any women with top-level decision making authority involved in these negotiations. The answer can be best illustrated in the images below: = (Photo by Jacquelyn Martin - Pool/Getty Images) = A photograph released by Pakistan’s government showing the Iranian delegation led by the speaker of Iran’s parliament, Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, second from right; and Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi, second from left. They were welcomed by Pakistan’s army chief, Asim Munir, left; and Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar, right, in Islamabad, Pakistan, on Friday.Credit...Pakistan's Ministry of Foreign Affairs, via Reuters This is the way it is, but it’s not the way it has to be. Many of you are writing to us expressing fear that we are living in drastically troubling times. In drastic times, we need drastic change: Starting with having MANY MORE women in the rooms where decisions about, literally, world peace are being made. According to data collected from international research between 1989-2011, when women are involved in peace agreements, peace is 35% more likely to last 15+ years. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | = | | = | = | | = | = | | = Women rarely have a seat at the table as men across the world are failing to achieve peace. name: Elissa Slotkin
subject: Does Trump want a free and fair election?
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ELISSA SLOTKIN FOR MICHIGAN
body:
# [](https://act.elissaslotkin.org/go/188?t=1&akid=2443%2E135344%2EQdfo1q)

I get a lot of questions in Michigan and across the country about whether or not Donald Trump wants a free and fair midterm election.
I think the answer is clear -- he is telling us exactly what he thinks, and he is already acting on it.
In places like Texas, he said he was "entitled" to five seats, trying to rig the next House election through partisan gerrymandering before a single vote was cast.
He has already tried to get voter rolls, has said that we should federalize elections, and would not rule out ICE and the National Guard at the polls.
And he has said exactly what he said before the 2020 elections -- that if his party doesn't win the midterms, the elections are rigged.
**[So what do we do? What keeps us safe? Please watch here.](https://act.elissaslotkin.org/go/4029?t=2&akid=2443%2E135344%2EQdfo1q)**
[](https://act.elissaslotkin.org/go/4029?t=3&akid=2443%2E135344%2EQdfo1q)
The only things that keep us safe in a democracy are free speech and fair elections. We must do all we can to protect both.
Elissa Slotkin
**[CONTRIBUTE NOW](https://act.elissaslotkin.org/donate/donate-today/?refcode=em260417-ak&t=4&akid=2443%2E135344%2EQdfo1q)**

PAID FOR BY ELISSA SLOTKIN FOR MICHIGAN
P.O. Box 4145
East Lansing, MI 48826
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a very important way we communicate with supporters like you, but if you want to, you can [ unsubscribe here](https://act.elissaslotkin.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe?t=5&akid=2443%2E135344%2EQdfo1q).

[ Sign up here to receive fewer emails.](https://act.elissaslotkin.org/signup/users-fewer-emails/?t=6&akid=2443%2E135344%2EQdfo1q)

Elissa Slotkin served in the Central Intelligence Agency and the Department of Defense. Use of her job titles and photographs during service do not imply endorsement by the Central Intelligence Agency OR the Department of Defense.

It’s fighters like you that help launch winning campaigns. From the bottom of my heart,** THANK YOU.** **Peter****, it’s Nancy Mace.** **[You’re receiving this because you supported my campaign for South Carolina Governor from DAY 1.](https://links.iterable.com/s/c/XQyabOPzvdoNrAYTrvmAyCQZAZHSC2cw0bNMY2Ze57AfV8DQagTj7ir4Yy-nUlJF8OfbE3VSm1_q_vexNDkydU-5XoLOWfMm3H0R1DAk4akNRKLnCAgej41bIsNbLk2CJshA8wkpAR_Rn0-I8RWDPwuKvFMhLSjc-vrvdB2aSa-oZGTKpIy1UDc1vk4lkGDw_V8vqMdm1_0zmsRHCFDJPExbCNMuBIKoG_uyixUIliKip9j3--QKTYNSotM8j2YcFd7AFwavo-Yd_XgY27RppXYQIG6N_9TzjJ0ZnzQHBYI1NzG_WQLtgMbr8_k4N2ywb45KelFpEqkIN18O1vhvfMWTl6U1pRKa3CblDwkp5YbPWgQTgdMpQlDjYb-8H3j8YyASTLaJtDoi4RhtcUKTDSkHf4sx_2S-g5i6layuVX7_n1jGgsJlXf0Q-xmyfuvntdTiybIS5_m-fsVL7cPFq8zI0cJabkbvrJI7gG-7XQ6UhcpqZTw-G3QOMMyPqywQ1Zy4YeiTYTVzrSXzibqSKt4gDnQDVQge-jMBiZlfyeMZcjwrpholqdgGfXKJagS_BFpUo50bowpitAuErEEtT3On6VtpfnndQleZksM8wKnhe4T45pSmFeneQdARThBJXWsECj4_egqSH6Q0GtBTk2yAEFpsFOPMClcp2Jqv2DZ9Zn8CJgPMIt3sJldZ5qwS8glgOqHKe-7l7slJJjkx_SgEJHJZqZKrGVAYzdTYdmps8GgRypPQ9AC6hVdp1YmqArZaTxajaveT6mXzNnXJBn89ttc9wNOv2JMBOP4OFjofs5OVIUE-RQyuMw5XWkkbwrcN8fwOtejqcAhFWhNYnixuz9rSVlguvu5BLmPaWysP5bLD_D_h2KilsDBgLdxcBNKKfhef8vhwg19JESR3h7dcdNFCTSWGkxjrir5S9vaCHGuaw6DbTdyWGbaZ50wFS0hV6BjKkUwWtvW2_w_8hgOJfWBpkGRYkNhGOeparkS84uDw6w8gqOPO6sRKqL6GdLlRmBOQqd--wefhepI6M5y_UxaZ-s5sH9wAkm2D-W0cXCDfmpmMIteJ47LN5EWeMoJ6U_Qc_UFketyAKN69ZH6floFkc-RCv5zI8SgZtA6vcNJLXvsr_oL90_uWuh3euw2-rbQUWD9ShheEpVhheHKYwsf6/i-HbRB8ZsWBYMrp6595lm8hreGOE8z3W/19)** I want to convey how much this means to me, because I wasn’t supposed to make it this far... Let me explain. **At 17, I dropped out of high school. **My parents, a tough Army General and an even tougher schoolteacher, gave me two choices: **Go back to school, or get a job.** So I went to work at a fine-dining establishment _**some of you may know as Waffle House.**_

| | | | High school dropout. Waffle House waitress. First woman to graduate from The Citadel. Single mom. Fighter.

Nancy Mace wasn’t born into power. She worked for every inch of ground she’s gained.

At 17, she dropped out of high school and took a job slinging hash browns at Waffle House to survive. No silver spoon. No Ivy League connections. Just grit, grace, and a whole lot of graveyard shifts.

By the grace of God, she turned that struggle into a story no politician can touch. She made history as the first woman to graduate from The Citadel, South Carolina’s legendary military college. She built her own business. She raised two kids on her own. And when the Left came for everything she believed in, she stood her ground. Now, she’s running for Governor of South Carolina.

Nancy Mace is a battle-tested conservative who’s walked through fire and come out swinging. In Congress, she’s been one of President Trump’s fiercest allies, fighting to secure our border, protect our daughters, and stop the Left’s sick agenda dead in its tracks.

She’s not here to make friends. She’s here to make history.

Nancy is fighting to:

* Eliminate South Carolina’s Income Tax—the highest in the Southeast

* Prosecute violent criminals and pedophiles with zero leniency

* Crack down on sanctuary sheriffs who let illegal aliens roam free

* Defund radical public universities that indoctrinate, not educate

* Reform education so students learn how to think—not what to think

* Work hand-in-hand with President Trump to put South Carolina FIRST

Through every hardship, Nancy never gave up. And now she’s ready to do what she’s always done: roll up her sleeves, take the heat, and lead from the front.

South Carolina is worth fighting for. And Nancy Mace is just getting started.
---

clean_body: | | | | | | | | It’s fighters like you that help launch winning campaigns. From the bottom of my heart, THANK YOU. Peter, it’s Nancy Mace. I want to convey how much this means to me, because I wasn’t supposed to make it this far... Let me explain. At 17, I dropped out of high school. My parents, a tough Army General and an even tougher schoolteacher, gave me two choices: Go back to school, or get a job. So I went to work at a fine-dining establishment some of you may know as Waffle House. Simply put, I’ve had to work for everything I have ever had in life. Jesus carried me through trauma, heartbreak, single motherhood, public failure, and private grief. He carried me when I was diagnosed with PTSD. He carried me when I moved four times to keep my children safe. And when I had nothing left to give, He filled me with living water. Twenty-five years ago, I made history as the first woman to graduate from The Citadel. I got here the hard way. And I’ve been FIGHTING ever since: 💥 I go toe-to-toe with Fake News CNN anchors on live TV.💥 I fight every single day for President Trump’s agenda. 💥 I’ve suffered DEATH THREATS for fighting to keep MEN out of women’s bathrooms. This isn’t about headlines. I’ve had a target on my back since I took on the establishment, but now they’re serious. They will stop at nothing to silence me. Dems are hurling MILLIONS against me, and establishment Republicans are throwing their support behind RINOs just to shut me down. The battle is just getting started. Hey there, Several weeks ago, we made a difficult decision. We decided it was time to make our move and launch a seven-figure ad campaign about my work standing up to Trump, the crises we face, and our fight for families working toward the California Dream. The good news is it worked -- we moved at just the right time. Slowly, we started to rise in the polls and in the last week we tripled our numbers and are now one of the favorites to win this race. The bad news is, we devoted a huge amount of our budget to that buy and those funds are running out fast. So now I am coming to you for help. Not for me, but for us. For this campaign. For anyone who understands the importance of California having a governor they can trust to fight for them. Because I cannot do it alone. There is only one way we'll keep two Republicans from making the general, and there's only one way we'll win this race -- and that is together. So please: [**Can I count on you to make a contribution to my campaign for Governor of California today? Our average online donation this past week is around $30. Many give more. Some give less. Will you please help us fight back? Our grassroots power will prevail, but only with your help.

This is the reality we’re facing this year, Peter: **More corporate and dark money than EVER is flooding into our elections.**
We’re talking BILLIONS of dollars expected to be spent by the corporate elites responsible for stripping our communities for parts. All to help the corrupt politicians blindly supporting the anti-American, anti-worker agenda that puts all of us in danger.
**That’s why we’re asking for your help today, Peter.** With your support, we’ve already proved that we’re able to build a record-breaking grassroots movement. President Donald Trump **[took on a swarm of reporters](https://substack.com/redirect/3d2b0aa1-7c1c-4c49-8442-8d5e60ed3ce2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** asking a bombardment of questions about the status of a peace deal with Iran and Pope's Leo's criticism of Trump's military operations.
"Did you just hear that? They're gonna execute a woman! Tell that to the Pope!" Trump fired back at one reporter, referring to Iran's scheduled **[execution of a female protester.](https://substack.com/redirect/5797eec8-b7d9-4c33-bf37-54e1aea351ac?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
