name: David Hogg
subject: The rot goes deeper than Trump
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY LEADERS WE DESERVE, LEADERSWEDESERVE
body:
Every U.S. bomb that falls on Iran puts money in someone’s pocket. And you deserve to know who.
# [](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/852?refcode=em20260418-1&t=1&akid=3384%2E587540%2EObP-58)
Peter,
Every U.S. bomb that falls on Iran puts money in someone’s pocket. And you deserve to know who.
Because the rot in our democracy goes much deeper than Trump.
**The congressional members responsible for authorizing hundreds of billions of dollars in military spending every single year are personally invested in the companies making the bombs that tear countries apart.**
**[If you’re as furious about that as I am, pitch in $5 right now.](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/2160?c0=em_ms_lwd_20260418_1_arms.PIBiGY&refcode=em20260418-3384&amount=5&t=2&akid=3384%2E587540%2EObP-58)**
**[Donate $5 »](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/2160?c0=em_ms_lwd_20260418_1_arms.PIBiGY&amount=5&refcode=em20260418-3384&t=3&akid=3384%2E587540%2EObP-58)**
Campaign finance records show that members of the Armed Services Committee are some of the top recipients of weapons industry donations. Corporations like Lockheed Martin, Boeing, Northrop Grumman, and Raytheon are funneling money directly to the representatives that control their contracts. Some congressmembers, or their families, own stock in those same companies.
Since the start of the year, Lockheed Martin stock is up 25%. Northrop Grumman is up 20%. That’s because the weapons industry doesn’t just survive the chaos. It thrives in it. And some members of Congress have made sure they’re along for the ride.
Sen. John Hickenlooper holds anywhere between $100,000 and $250,000 worth of stock in RTX, the weapons megacorporation formerly known as Raytheon. Sen. Susan Collins’ husband holds stock in Boeing and RTX. Sen. Shelley Moore Capito holds Lockheed Martin stock.
The very people who get to decide if we go to war or not, greenlight a new weapons program, or increase an already massively bloated defense budget, could be simultaneously voting to increase the value of their own portfolio.
They should not have financial conflicts of interest in how those decisions get made, and it should be illegal for companies that stand to profit the most from war to fund our politicians' campaigns. **We’re talking about the people voting on where your tax dollars will go — and lining their pockets in the process.**
That is the antithesis of public service, and a violation of our democracy so egregious it should be front page news every single day.
And yet, here we are.
This is exactly the kind of corrupt, broken system that Leaders We Deserve was built to fight against — by electing leaders who refuse to treat working people like pawns on a chessboard. Leaders who won’t take money from the very corporations that stand to make trillions from endless war. Who don’t own stock in the industries they are supposed to regulate. Who show up every single day to protect their communities, not defense contractors.
This is the fight. Not just beating Republicans up and down the ballot in the midterms, but building a Democratic Party that has a backbone. Leaders who will fight back against corporate power, the billionaire class, and every other special interest who thinks they can buy their way into our democracy — without flinching.
And we’re the ones who are going to build that.
**[Will you pitch in $5 today to help Leaders We Deserve continue backing young, fearless progressives who will put power back into the hands of working people?](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/2160?c0=em_ms_lwd_20260418_1_arms.PIBiGY&refcode=em20260418-3384&amount=5&t=4&akid=3384%2E587540%2EObP-58)**
**[Donate $5 »](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/2160?c0=em_ms_lwd_20260418_1_arms.PIBiGY&amount=5&refcode=em20260418-3384&t=23&akid=3384%2E587540%2EObP-58)**
Thank you,
David Hogg
* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lwd-em-footer?refcode=em20260418-1&t=24&refcode2=3384_587540_ObP-58&akid=3384%2E587540%2EObP-58)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=25&akid=3384%2E587540%2EObP-58)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/signup/update-your-information/)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=26&akid=3384%2E587540%2EObP-58)

To give by check:
Leaders We Deserve
611 Pennsylvania Ave SE
#192
Washington, DC 20003
Email us: [info@leaderswedeserve.com](mailto:info@leaderswedeserve.com)

name: Dr. Amy Acton
subject: I worked for a Republican Governor. Now I’m running to be a Democratic Governor.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Maggie for NH
body:
We need to put partisan politics aside to help the people of Ohio.

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/129717182/615298755/76101138?amount=10&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2026.04.18_Amy-Acton-Split_B1_F1_S1_C1_90OP_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODg1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0NmU2NzRiOS0zNzNiLWYxMTEtOGVmMi0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=1bFkyvr515lZVjZ2R_uSOs8PoJbajo0xh4qzUshG8rM=) I’m Dr. Amy Acton, and I’m the Democratic candidate for governor of Ohio.

I grew up in Youngstown, Ohio, struggling through abuse, neglect, and hunger — and even spent one winter living in a tent.

I often share that public schools saved my life. After pulling myself out of poverty, I worked three jobs to put myself through med school.

Throughout my career, I’ve always worked with both sides of the aisle — because I’ll work with anyone who wants to solve problems instead of making them. In 2019, I was appointed by a Republican governor to lead the Ohio Department of Health. I held drug companies accountable, kept communities healthy, and was called to serve during the most unprecedented public health crisis of our lifetimes.

I’m a doctor, not a politician, but I am a public servant.

**I’m running for governor because people in my state are struggling, and I refuse to look the other way while bad actors and special interests keep taking Ohio backwards on nearly every measure.**

The problems we face don’t care if you’re a Democrat or a Republican. They’re just problems that need to be fixed. And I don’t care if you’re a Democrat or a Republican, either. I’ll fight for you and your family just the same.

_But I can’t win this race alone; that’s why I’m asking for your help today._

We’re up against a Trump-endorsed, MAGA billionaire backed by other out-of-state billionaires. My opponent, Vivek Ramaswamy, just dropped **$10 MILLION** in this race to try and defeat me because he knows Ohioans aren’t falling for the MAGA playbook.

name: Gina Hinojosa
subject: I took a leap of faith, Peter.
body:
This is one of the most important lessons I’ve learned.
# [](https://ginafortexas.com/?refcode=em_ms_gh_header)[](https://ginafortexas.com/?refcode=em_ms_gh_header)
[](https://act.ginafortexas.com/go/625?c0=em_ms_gh_20260418_1_leap.vTG-5-&refcode=em20260418-941&amount=5&t=1&akid=941%2E299639%2E4fWzDI)
Peter,
I learned one of the most important lessons of my life on the floor of the Texas House — more specifically, the lesson came the moment I chose to _walk off of it._
In 2021, the GOP was ramming through an extreme anti-voter bill. I felt something rising in me that I couldn’t ignore. I knew staying put meant letting Texans lose their freedom to vote.
**So I took a leap of faith.**
I stood up, texted other legislators:
_“A leap of faith only works if you take it.”_
Together, we ran out the clock and stopped a bill designed to silence Texas voters. That quorum break protected Sunday morning voting — a sacred tradition in Black churches — and blocked a provision that would have allowed partisan judges to overturn election results without evidence. And we earned that victory together.
So I’ll repeat that to you today:
**A leap of faith only works if you take it.**
And when you do, others will leap with you.
**I’m taking another leap of faith with this campaign, Peter.**
Texas is on another precipice. Our schools are under attack, our freedoms are eroding, and right-wing corporate donors have more influence in the Governor’s Office than everyday Texans ever will unless we change course immediately.
If Texans take a leap of faith together next year, I know we can win this fight.
**[Can I count on you to donate $5 now to power my grassroots campaign?](https://act.ginafortexas.com/go/625?c0=em_ms_gh_20260418_1_leap.vTG-5-&refcode=em20260418-941&amount=5&t=2&akid=941%2E299639%2E4fWzDI)**
[Donate $5 »](https://act.ginafortexas.com/go/625?c0=em_ms_gh_20260418_1_leap.vTG-5-&amount=5&refcode=em20260418-941&t=21&akid=941%2E299639%2E4fWzDI)
No te dejes.
— Gina

[ Donate » ](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_gh_footer?refcode=em20260418-1&t=22&refcode2=941_299639_4fWzDI&akid=941%2E299639%2E4fWzDI)
[ Donate Monthly » ](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_gh_footer?refcode=em20260418-1&recurring=1&t=23&refcode2=941_299639_4fWzDI&akid=941%2E299639%2E4fWzDI)

name: Senator Johnson
subject: Kill the filibuster. Pack the court. Make DC a state. This is how they lock in power forever.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Ron Johnson for Senate Inc
body:
| | | | [](http://links.g4yamerica.com/u/click?_t=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_m=ccd196ed3dcc4944b4bc2eaaa6fe77c8&_e=KFFRtFxBbO6jRzLBAc4G8N1BH_wEP7kslkTvVOJ8iX88yuXxxytqVY_bqr4hFUny3Gm9RAZ42J8r7ckJYlkzoQBAQv4S4tVoMmEUGp6maza5MLIPjUWe2n4Zw1iRsA2SciLWmUTeCVCA1wNtK21z6agKy2ok4VZUMYhF9UYUKPA4vxJKb_uc0LFGhbj0U4CVtCeYaFNDxz_0R9NBXbmdGOdMbZ2Nq2A9fIy6XsZFQDmJOFge4QniTVL3dEoq3ReqnkzDzsshyPcLqTWBG86FSysMuW5V5RSWVZNAE7h_78EUlJyzVlyWFml4l7oSVgmB-feL3eW8AVbvwQdSaMQ9_vOYU3cwGUGFAwMAgryfWVLkWqh-EBLsEZOauMqDvvBEV7faKxInPyD53iqVKfmGFPpM4vPJrYsDHIMxtvrXfw6dampOZ64oWi7Hcoa7R5W-c6OANm1PyumSm8nJf1t2ZTgyJ_vQwFWFrXCm1ilHfWfgCu6-sO-KF029E-UdWn7bdJgi1mBi6hpNPmFYrhHdw8rxhVJmrO8k899wLOI34fKarzlFjc0gXptI__ONipJBb3-ohKLePXVMibKpMv-eurbjQVur12J7agaUDq1v66cb0tGSUYN9ajO7Niv9dskh)
---

Friend,
The Left is on a mission to take back the Senate, and there’s no coming back from what they have planned.
It's no secret –
* _**STEP ONE: Eliminate the filibuster.**_ Simple majority rule replaces the 60-vote threshold that forces the Senate to actually build consensus.
* _**STEP TWO: Pack the Supreme Court.**_ Add four, five, six new justices — all chosen by a Democrat president, confirmed by a simple majority, and the constitutional guardrails we've spent decades rebuilding dismantled overnight.
* _**STEP THREE: Make D.C. a state.**_ Two new Democrat senators, automatically, permanently. They lock in their majority for a generation.

This isn’t hyperbole. It’s the agenda they’ve said OUT LOUD.
**_[Knowing all this, I have to ask you an important question: Will you donate today to STOP the Left’s permanent power grab?](http://links.g4yamerica.com/u/click?_t=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_m=ccd196ed3dcc4944b4bc2eaaa6fe77c8&_e=KFFRtFxBbO6jRzLBAc4G8N1BH_wEP7kslkTvVOJ8iX88yuXxxytqVY_bqr4hFUny3Gm9RAZ42J8r7ckJYlkzoQBAQv4S4tVoMmEUGp6maza5MLIPjUWe2n4Zw1iRsA2SciLWmUTeCVCA1wNtK21z6agKy2ok4VZUMYhF9UYUKPA4vxJKb_uc0LFGhbj0U4CVtCeYaFNDxz_0R9NBXbmdGOA9PolPqPlooy6rKdCQzoWzQH7PSEHWy7dsEOjKnaLFruDIKesKCW_2zp9b4iNfZ_RE6NnpBvQPmDBSdZs_SavMH73Rbgb0X1A_OOOX2axmywrB0o_kw_GPRyjT9pxqn302zaRAwNmYKJ6nwZONrFHq_SHoltqC6Lkqw-f0v1yGMbrK0b04AwNKcfqHnwVTz7g-ee6IbO-rf5RHmeolJpXzbi7RVpTo1SfMaXkElaDdHEtMGFmRAGZXA81vbgDLuOZAY2Cb2qRWwViyXr_Z_fWmWfy2xtfSdMcBLcJRcdk92eWhtleu1WMNx-q81L8Gh6jxT9zAztoXrxamG_YHTpBSk6zQCYDKyuqJ_SSAHanoGYpm-eMjg5FkKOwfrXFvZM8AIAL0M5ERcBZmzJrp4CtgZ5ZZLoWgiZGeoZ3467bL)_**
The only thing standing between their radical wishlist and reality is Republican control of the Senate.
I've heard from a lot of voters lately who are frustrated. Angry, even.
Some are disenchanted. Some feel like nothing ever really changes — that we win elections and then watch the Establishment fumble it.
_**I understand that frustration. I share it.**_
I've spent my career in the Senate fighting a bureaucracy that fights back, a media that never gives us a fair shot, and a Democrat Party that plays for keeps in a way our side too often doesn't.
But here's what I know: Sitting it out doesn't punish the politicians you're angry at.
It hands power to the people who will destroy our country as we know it.
**[Are you willing to pitch in $5, $10, or $15 if it means stopping the Left’s permanent power grab?](http://links.g4yamerica.com/u/click?_t=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_m=ccd196ed3dcc4944b4bc2eaaa6fe77c8&_e=KFFRtFxBbO6jRzLBAc4G8N1BH_wEP7kslkTvVOJ8iX88yuXxxytqVY_bqr4hFUny3Gm9RAZ42J8r7ckJYlkzoQBAQv4S4tVoMmEUGp6maza5MLIPjUWe2n4Zw1iRsA2SciLWmUTeCVCA1wNtK21z6agKy2ok4VZUMYhF9UYUKPA4vxJKb_uc0LFGhbj0U4CVtCeYaFNDxz_0R9NBXbmdGBWYbwCjAHAKAdRGYbY1xIpuKSetDydJwsw4NY-SfG3gzKEqSjNozlrKvK_EGvmehCu4ya7zhrtF4Hp1ytSg27vOjNGNI4lzarxxMXse-uxMCY7EtgxeL7ezZScKPm-vDe-bCGVZvmCPo1y9Uk5d6XiEgdRE9sWO8AxPtRiHW9gV6UHm-5VFF8unv8uLEuQPXAFO0RE_bJhgqTVKhHZ2sLbBcLnDGYkImkSstf3E8o7xfxeAhViuGBIEdGzHQaoMRLyKjSy03li-B23YTKshM-B_s7-pN-aHZju7ZegWqQFGk63BOEb9aOB-4-w_5Qqc2fvkHHU0dByoudHRR_ont4GxGpExQu2Yv8GjrHImpaeH-nVAnJgwQGmXf9qtgreHFV8mXkwMT4rrqMcw9NjcZ0QEquS-c9fkC3V-2bPwD9GI)**
I've been through brutal campaigns. I've had union bosses, liberal billionaires, and out-of-state activists pour tens of millions of dollars into defeating me.
**They failed because grassroots conservatives like you showed up.**
That's all I'm asking. Make a contribution. Get off the sidelines. Don't let the Left win by default.

name: Rashida Tlaib
subject: Re: This Black Maternal Health Week: Stand up for Black moms now.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rashida Tlaib for Congress
body:
Save lives and build a more equitable world by supporting the Michigan Chamber for Reproductive Justice today. | | | [](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.X6XUC7u8ppFLNtMO_55SZlNseb7gEYIOhm_EWnhzEOZKmTtnV3k2h4V2x_O3H7rdW2az5rgktBwzxQ8Ml9Wgkmv85qW-UsBonL4KRa0bcNqdICU6T3NBDdNIZG7w7wEWBPMvaUlDdUgkmhpSEsVFTasB5bok_iH_77hpPMKPf9ZyAu5RPQ7WjQGTiq3V7Dkh0b2SMvjQqfc_SR0TWuWqsEZpaE6RFhhI6zwVs-o4luj5jRIpQQrqwAYMBIX4TYikkyXcg0-mO5A4q2UUCBpx6hR0ggdfEMt_9H8MJVIx457nOjydxjX53kis54drlFY0Bra9WcxUM5VyB1KCmywP6ZwZpA2pbd_eBt8I88xDcKoeNGJq4t9dpYnvbZ07CfRR/4pw/7KseLeGLT5e-XlcaZCIYbw/h0/h001.9IlvnPzVnOQHJHiXAGcdtR7JmMvq8bbe7AD-YU5OqCg)

Friend, You often hear me talk about the need to be “rooted in community”—to always center the needs of our communities, whether through activism or policymaking. This Black Maternal Health Week, we celebrated the resilience of Black families—focusing on community-driven solutions for healing, equity, and reproductive justice with the theme, “Rooted in Justice & Joy.” The **Michigan Chamber for Reproductive Justice** (MCRJ) exemplifies this, leading the push for transformative legislation that addresses the root causes of maternal health disparities in our state, ensuring that all people receive the dignified, equitable care they deserve before, during, and after birth. **But due to federal cuts and funding freezes, MCRJ has become more dependent on grassroots donations to advocate for and support the communities they serve.** [**This Black Maternal Health Week, will you join me in supporting the Michigan Chamber for Reproductive Justice with a donation of $5 or or more today?**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqsXu0R1XDWRVJnEMC9_ePsJk1mZ834WYhJDLS2P_zfAXG0wgplUfSThfBt_4Cj29kIykkoIDQy91iBpOM3aPCycnmeZXVqAL7INbOBm8x_IjuWp-ej_B8oNAPC5kPHFbwc4wTRWuJDFZ10a14MKcSw0OfPQQQLZGMyryzIYCefSTgfNnoa5dVt5XdFXgXITIjnMPWFea6WXU4lPRySGYx-JxVI6SW8282wvL1cOhVSj3zKbwb-BwWp36zZqt1ZOIptp6dbJtuX04ZQNIJ27JZAET0Wp9W9IJayGplCJUFPE7e5L7Fb20AXeEJQKOdMLikDg79wH7n3htbcNKkpDkzmKVuvqR7YQav8ibCEcsUVIPohQESO8-EdKjIevX4AcVRrI4YYlBRkpkHn4UY9ZX9-f/4pw/7KseLeGLT5e-XlcaZCIYbw/h1/h001.ReEJ7R8EIDh7540o6353H0eJugMtltevb8B-ePE30UA) | [SUPPORT MCRJ](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqsXu0R1XDWRVJnEMC9_ePsJk1mZ834WYhJDLS2P_zfAXNauryzu2xdiNe8dtVgECQHcNEwq7IQoFbDSh2IQisu7V4OmdkcPEXZmtUDHKRBJWQQ6Amf0QAF1Wz18Is8cMSESuONoXFz3xMqI8A-qTWrGFWQkWsbYQAOqvVA6lPUSrugM00vTdemKV4t3wXGezsAZOZAl4Ew4TO8T4ipfXcn7rSJtWXucbZaeHVKpoyVvxNCIDzs0Eo3rRew4aqVYDRam60sheZ4eN61XTQS2zwNFlog3hylZAXu7HU8UH5RqL18cGl5BoGWBV9q9YkBOWc_y1i0mHuXIYPVAFLDp0l1NW-0zCuLdXmj7kJhgvQrQ4n0ad5ZxynBNHQ9j-uE7kNVftAwAHURCd9QC_jnUm-0U/4pw/7KseLeGLT5e-XlcaZCIYbw/h2/h001.2eT0GUXTCtcrGq6S5H-RpWk_N1gFF0ZkwnbcT3G1NRI)

MCRJ’s work is urgently needed in Michigan, which has one of the nation’s highest Black maternal mortality rates. Here, Black women are more than 4.5 times more likely to die of pregnancy-related causes than white women. These deaths are heartbreaking and preventable.
**As the policy lead in a statewide coalition for birth justice, the Michigan Chamber for Reproductive Justice is advancing much-needed solutions to save lives.**
The group’s relentless advocacy led to:
* A law to expand access to birth centers, which are proven to improve Black maternal health outcomes
* The introduction and advancement of the “Michigan Momnibus” policy package, including bills to protect patients and reimburse doulas and midwives, which has passed the Michigan State Senate and is now in the State House
* Direct support and critical resources for Black birthing people, their families, and healthcare providers

In addition to policy progress for birth justice, the MCRJ is fighting for water affordability, housing justice, environmental protections, person-centered affirming care, and more. **MCRJ recognizes that these intersecting issues are essential to achieving true reproductive justice and freedom.**
Right now, right-wing attacks on healthcare access and funding for reproductive health and maternal health are threatening MCRJ’s progress.
These attacks and cuts to funding are jeopardizing the safety net for marginalized communities, who already face significant barriers to quality healthcare. The Michigan Chamber for Reproductive Justice’s work depends on federal resources to expand access to doulas, midwives, and culturally competent care. Without sustained federal funding, they’re forced to rely on grassroots donations to ensure communities don’t lose access to life-saving support.

Subject: This Black Maternal Health Week: Stand up for Black moms now.

Friend,
In my state of Michigan, Black maternal mortality rates are among the highest in the nation—with Black women 4.5 times more likely to die from pregnancy-related causes than white women, and Black infants 3 times more likely to die than white infants.
Solutions to this urgent crisis include investing in more Black healthcare providers and more birth centers, which are midwife-led, home-like places that have been proven to improve maternal and infant health outcomes.
**The Michigan Chamber for Reproductive Justice (MCRJ) is advancing Black maternal health through grassroots leadership, policy advocacy, and coalition building.** MCRJ is committed to ensuring that all people receive the dignified, equitable care they deserve before, during, and after birth.
As the policy lead for the Michigan State of Birth Justice Coalition, MCRJ grounds its work in the foundational leadership of Birth Detroit—home to the only Black-led birth center in the state. The coalition’s community-rooted vision has helped shape critical policy wins, including a newly signed law to license and expand access to birth centers, and the “Michigan Momnibus” policy package—featuring vital bills to support doulas and midwives and protect patients from discrimination.

Thanks in part to the relentless advocacy from the Michigan Chamber for Reproductive Justice, the Michigan State Senate introduced and passed the “Michigan Momnibus” package during last year’s Black Maternal Health Week. Now MCRJ is working to demand a vote in the Michigan State House.
While pushing for life-saving policies, the Michigan Chamber for Reproductive Justice is also providing education and resources to communities, families, healthcare providers, and the institutions that shape their care experiences.
This community group’s work is rooted in reproductive justice, which is the human right to maintain personal bodily autonomy, to have children or not have children, and to parent in safe, sustainable communities.
In addition to birth justice, MCRJ advocates for intersecting issues such as environmental justice and housing justice—recognizing that access to clean water, safe housing, and economic stability is essential to true reproductive freedom. This includes women who are birthing while in custody, where autonomy and care are often constrained by carceral systems.
The Michigan Chamber for Reproductive Justice’s critical work depends on federal resources to expand access to doulas, midwives, and culturally competent care. **Cuts by conservatives in Congress and illegal funding freezes by this administration make the need for grassroots donations even more critical to ensure that communities don’t lose access to life-saving support.**

Thank you. Our systems and institutions have failed to provide Black people with the high-quality healthcare that everyone deserves. **But I refuse to accept this status quo, and I thank you for standing with me and the movement for health equity.**
In Congress, I’m supporting policies for Black maternal and infant health like the federal _Momnibus Act_ , and I’m working with the Congressional Mamas’ Caucus to center Black mothers and other mothers of color in policymaking.
Together, we will continue demanding and building a society that honors everyone’s safety, dignity, and bodily autonomy.
In love and solidarity,
Rashida

clean_body: Save lives and build a more equitable world by supporting the Michigan Chamber for Reproductive Justice today. | | | Friend, You often hear me talk about the need to be “rooted in community”—to always center the needs of our communities, whether through activism or policymaking. This Black Maternal Health Week, we celebrated the resilience of Black families—focusing on community-driven solutions for healing, equity, and reproductive justice with the theme, “Rooted in Justice & Joy.” The Michigan Chamber for Reproductive Justice (MCRJ) exemplifies this, leading the push for transformative legislation that addresses the root causes of maternal health disparities in our state, ensuring that all people receive the dignified, equitable care they deserve before, during, and after birth. But due to federal cuts and funding freezes, MCRJ has become more dependent on grassroots donations to advocate for and support the communities they serve. | MCRJ’s work is urgently needed in Michigan, which has one of the nation’s highest Black maternal mortality rates. Here, Black women are more than 4.5 times more likely to die of pregnancy-related causes than white women. These deaths are heartbreaking and preventable. As the policy lead in a statewide coalition for birth justice, the Michigan Chamber for Reproductive Justice is advancing much-needed solutions to save lives. The group’s relentless advocacy led to: A law to expand access to birth centers, which are proven to improve Black maternal health outcomes The introduction and advancement of the “Michigan Momnibus” policy package, including bills to protect patients and reimburse doulas and midwives, which has passed the Michigan State Senate and is now in the State House Direct support and critical resources for Black birthing people, their families, and healthcare providers If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your secure donation will go through immediately, with 100% going directly to Michigan Chamber for Reproductive Justice | | | | | | | | | In addition to policy progress for birth justice, the MCRJ is fighting for water affordability, housing justice, environmental protections, person-centered affirming care, and more. MCRJ recognizes that these intersecting issues are essential to achieving true reproductive justice and freedom. Right now, right-wing attacks on healthcare access and funding for reproductive health and maternal health are threatening MCRJ’s progress. These attacks and cuts to funding are jeopardizing the safety net for marginalized communities, who already face significant barriers to quality healthcare. The Michigan Chamber for Reproductive Justice’s work depends on federal resources to expand access to doulas, midwives, and culturally competent care. Without sustained federal funding, they’re forced to rely on grassroots donations to ensure communities don’t lose access to life-saving support. Thank you for taking action to build more safety and equity for Black women and all people. Together, we will fight back against racism, fascism, and dehumanizing hate. We will also keep pushing toward positive visions of the world we all deserve, including this one from a convening of reproductive justice leaders: “Our vision is a future rooted in human dignity and worth, bodily autonomy, joy, love, and rest.”1 In solidarity, Rashida 1 \---------- Forwarded message --------- From: Rashida Tlaib**** Date: Fri, Apr 17, 2026 Subject: This Black Maternal Health Week: Stand up for Black moms now. To: dpwillis67@gmail.com Friend, In my state of Michigan, Black maternal mortality rates are among the highest in the nation—with Black women 4.5 times more likely to die from pregnancy-related causes than white women, and Black infants 3 times more likely to die than white infants. Solutions to this urgent crisis include investing in more Black healthcare providers and more birth centers, which are midwife-led, home-like places that have been proven to improve maternal and infant health outcomes. The Michigan Chamber for Reproductive Justice (MCRJ) is advancing Black maternal health through grassroots leadership, policy advocacy, and coalition building. MCRJ is committed to ensuring that all people receive the dignified, equitable care they deserve before, during, and after birth. As the policy lead for the Michigan State of Birth Justice Coalition, MCRJ grounds its work in the foundational leadership of Birth Detroit—home to the only Black-led birth center in the state. The coalition’s community-rooted vision has helped shape critical policy wins, including a newly signed law to license and expand access to birth centers, and the “Michigan Momnibus” policy package—featuring vital bills to support doulas and midwives and protect patients from discrimination. Thanks in part to the relentless advocacy from the Michigan Chamber for Reproductive Justice, the Michigan State Senate introduced and passed the “Michigan Momnibus” package during last year’s Black Maternal Health Week. Now MCRJ is working to demand a vote in the Michigan State House. While pushing for life-saving policies, the Michigan Chamber for Reproductive Justice is also providing education and resources to communities, families, healthcare providers, and the institutions that shape their care experiences. This community group’s work is rooted in reproductive justice, which is the human right to maintain personal bodily autonomy, to have children or not have children, and to parent in safe, sustainable communities. In addition to birth justice, MCRJ advocates for intersecting issues such as environmental justice and housing justice—recognizing that access to clean water, safe housing, and economic stability is essential to true reproductive freedom. This includes women who are birthing while in custody, where autonomy and care are often constrained by carceral systems. The Michigan Chamber for Reproductive Justice’s critical work depends on federal resources to expand access to doulas, midwives, and culturally competent care. Cuts by conservatives in Congress and illegal funding freezes by this administration make the need for grassroots donations even more critical to ensure that communities don’t lose access to life-saving support. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your secure donation will go through immediately, with 100% going directly to Michigan Chamber for Reproductive Justice | | | | | | | | | Thank you. Our systems and institutions have failed to provide Black people with the high-quality healthcare that everyone deserves. But I refuse to accept this status quo, and I thank you for standing with me and the movement for health equity. In Congress, I’m supporting policies for Black maternal and infant health like the federal Momnibus Act , and I’m working with the Congressional Mamas’ Caucus to center Black mothers and other mothers of color in policymaking. Together, we will continue demanding and building a society that honors everyone’s safety, dignity, and bodily autonomy. In love and solidarity, Rashida * Rashida Tlaib for Congress PO Box 32777 Detroit, MI 48232 | Paid for by Rashida Tlaib for Congress | | Sent via . With your support we can harness the power of our grassroots supporters to influence decision makers and put control back in the hands of the people.

Can I tell you a little secret, Peter?

_I never intended to run for public office – especially not Governor._

I've been a physician for nearly 40 years. I thought that was my one and only career path.

**But when I saw the Statehouse place special interests above everyday Ohioans over and over again for decades, I decided I couldn't look away any longer.**

So no, I'm not a career politician. I'm a wife, a mother, a Youngstown native, and a preventive medicine doctor who knows all too well the tough circumstances that Ohioans are under right now.

I'm running for Governor because we don't need another out-of-touch billionaire running this state – we need someone _who gets it._

But there’s a reason people like me typically don’t get elected. I don’t have billionaire friends or corrupt special interests ready to pour millions into my campaign.

**Instead, I’m counting on hardworking folks like you, Peter, to ensure this campaign is fully equipped to take on Vivek Ramaswamy's warchest of MAGA money.**

[_**If you're with me, I need to humbly ask if you can support my campaign and Abigail Spanberger's United for Virginia's Future PAC with $10 in the next 30 minutes. >>**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/129718658/615303237/225388141?refcode=BLU_EM_UFVF_2026-04-18_1&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS8xMDM4NTgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMGIxMTBmYWQtMzkzYi1mMTExLThlZjItMDAwZDNhMTRiNjQwIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=v64D1OApffsUxROD3ZS4DsQh5fvekjDaYFFGCUAZfwg=)

clean_body: I never intended to run for public office – especially not Governor. I've been a physician for nearly 40 years. I thought that was my one and only career path. | = Can I tell you a little secret, Peter? I never intended to run for public office – especially not Governor. I've been a physician for nearly 40 years. I thought that was my one and only career path. But when I saw the Statehouse place special interests above everyday Ohioans over and over again for decades, I decided I couldn't look away any longer. So no, I'm not a career politician. I'm a wife, a mother, a Youngstown native, and a preventive medicine doctor who knows all too well the tough circumstances that Ohioans are under right now. I'm running for Governor because we don't need another out-of-touch billionaire running this state – we need someone who gets it. But there’s a reason people like me typically don’t get elected. I don’t have billionaire friends or corrupt special interests ready to pour millions into my campaign. Instead, I’m counting on hardworking folks like you, Peter, to ensure this campaign is fully equipped to take on Vivek Ramaswamy's warchest of MAGA money. = | | | = | = | = | = | = I couldn't do any of this without you. Be well, Dr. Amy Acton | | = Paid for by United for Virginia's Future PAC United for Virginia's Future PAC P.O. Box 3121 Glen Allen, VA 23058 United States *

name: Sharice Davids
subject: I’ve worked a lot of jobs in my life. My stint as an MMA fighter taught me some of my toughest lessons.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY SHARICE FOR CONGRESS
body:
Believe it or not, there are a lot of similarities between the Octagon and the Capitol

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/129718709/615303493/-1360420019?amounts=5,10,15,25,50,100,250&refcode=email_041826_MMA&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NTc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyYzFjMDA1Yi0zYTNiLWYxMTEtOGVmMi0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=l6D7Vo1UKGQSUHS1HfzWz8IYdGwjPklc878JbDXOUbI=) I’ve worked a lot of jobs throughout my life — Congresswoman, White House Fellow under President Obama, working in economic development for Native American communities, just to name a few. But the job where I learned some of my most valuable lessons was my time as a professional MMA fighter. Because believe it or not, there are a lot of similarities between the Octagon and the Capitol. But the biggest one is this: I’ve never won a fight alone. **_[Every fight I ever stepped into — just like every bill I’ve worked on in Congress — took a team behind me. So if we want to deliver a KO to the National GOP’s majority on Election Day, I need your help. Will you chip in just a few dollars right now to help us stay in this fight?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/129718710/615303515/-1360420019?amounts=5,10,15,25,50,100,250&refcode=email_041826_MMA&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NTc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyYzFjMDA1Yi0zYTNiLWYxMTEtOGVmMi0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=l6D7Vo1UKGQSUHS1HfzWz8IYdGwjPklc878JbDXOUbI=)_** | | [Donate →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/129718711/615303520/-1360420019?amounts=5,10,15,25,50,100,250&refcode=email_041826_MMA&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NTc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyYzFjMDA1Yi0zYTNiLWYxMTEtOGVmMi0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=l6D7Vo1UKGQSUHS1HfzWz8IYdGwjPklc878JbDXOUbI=)
---
Now I’ll be the first to admit — MMA isn’t the most traditional path to elected office. But as someone who’s thrown more than a few (literal!) punches throughout my life, I think Washington could learn a thing or two from fighters like me.
_Namely, you don’t win a fight by running scared and backing yourself into a corner._
And let’s be honest: That’s exactly what Donald Trump and his allies want us to do. They’re throwing punch after punch, hoping we lose focus, step back, and give up ground.
Trust me, I know it’s tempting — when someone’s throwing punches at you, it’s natural to retreat. But in my experience, that’s exactly when you lose control of the fight.
Instead, we need to do exactly what bullies like Trump don’t expect us to do: **Go on offense.**
I’ve fought a lot of people like Trump — people who thought they were on top of the world. So I want you to remember this: _That’s when they’re at their weakest._
That’s why we need to stay hopeful and focused. Not only on Election Day, but in all the days leading up to it, too. Because just like in MMA, fights like these aren’t decided in a single moment. They’re decided by the work you put in long before the final round.
**_[We have National Republicans on their heels, and all we need is you. Please, will you chip in and help me keep fighting for the government, the economy, and the future Kansas deserves?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/129718713/615303527/-1360420019?amounts=5,10,15,25,50,100,250&refcode=email_041826_MMA&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NTc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyYzFjMDA1Yi0zYTNiLWYxMTEtOGVmMi0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=l6D7Vo1UKGQSUHS1HfzWz8IYdGwjPklc878JbDXOUbI=)_**

clean_body: Believe it or not, there are a lot of similarities between the Octagon and the Capitol = I’ve worked a lot of jobs throughout my life — Congresswoman, White House Fellow under President Obama, working in economic development for Native American communities, just to name a few. But the job where I learned some of my most valuable lessons was my time as a professional MMA fighter. Because believe it or not, there are a lot of similarities between the Octagon and the Capitol. But the biggest one is this: I’ve never won a fight alone. = | | = Now I’ll be the first to admit — MMA isn’t the most traditional path to elected office. But as someone who’s thrown more than a few (literal!) punches throughout my life, I think Washington could learn a thing or two from fighters like me. Namely, you don’t win a fight by running scared and backing yourself into a corner. And let’s be honest: That’s exactly what Donald Trump and his allies want us to do. They’re throwing punch after punch, hoping we lose focus, step back, and give up ground. Trust me, I know it’s tempting — when someone’s throwing punches at you, it’s natural to retreat. But in my experience, that’s exactly when you lose control of the fight. Instead, we need to do exactly what bullies like Trump don’t expect us to do: Go on offense. I’ve fought a lot of people like Trump — people who thought they were on top of the world. So I want you to remember this: That’s when they’re at their weakest. That’s why we need to stay hopeful and focused. Not only on Election Day, but in all the days leading up to it, too. Because just like in MMA, fights like these aren’t decided in a single moment. They’re decided by the work you put in long before the final round. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | = | = | | = | = | | = | = With gratitude, Sharice | This email was sent to: dpwillis67@gmail.com. When I cast my vote for Barack Obama in 2008, I felt a surge of hope for the future. I felt certain that progress was on the horizon, and I was excited to be part of it. When we cast our votes, that's what we should feel.
We can still feel that way about government if we focus all our efforts on electing leaders who will put the people first.
Hope is not dead yet.
When we look back at this moment in history, we will all have to contend with what we did to stop the rise of authoritarianism in our country. One of the best ways we can do this is elect leaders in the midterm who will not back down from a fight. Let your legacy be one of standing up.

When we cast our votes, that's what we should feel. We can still feel that way about government if we focus all our efforts on electing leaders who will put the people first. Hope is not dead yet. When we look back at this moment in history, we will all have to contend with what we did to stop the rise of authoritarianism in our country. One of the best ways we can do this is elect leaders in the midterm who will not back down from a fight. Let your legacy be one of standing up. This is our LAST CHANCE to stop Trump — it won't come again. That's why I need you to make your voice heard. Will you split a donation today between my campaign and Resist Reclaim Rebuild to help build a future that works for all of us? [] CHIP IN TODAY TO HELP STOP TRUMP! [] Thank you, Jack [] Resist Reclaim Rebuild is an organization committed to helping Democrats win up and down the ballot, nationwide. PAID FOR BY RESIST RECLAIM REBUILD PAC (WWW.RESISTRECLAIMREBUILD.COM) AND NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE OR CANDIDATE COMMITTEE. Resist Reclaim Rebuild 1331 FLORIDA AVE NW Washington, DC 20009 United States If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: .

name: Virginians For Fair Elections
subject: Let’s finish what we started
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Virginians for Fair Elections
body:
# [](https://act.virginiansforfairelections.com/go/629?refcode=em20260418-533&t=1&akid=533%2E12592%2EI-rCvY)
A week ago, you helped us do something incredible:
Thousands of volunteers showed up and knocked on doors. It was the largest organizing day of this campaign, and it’s clear that Virginia felt it.
**Right now, the YES campaign for redistricting has a slim lead according to the polls** but it’s absolutely critical that we build on our momentum, protect our lead, and expand it in the final days before Election Day on April 21.
Today is the final day of early voting and our last weekend before Election Day. What we do right now will shape what happens on April 21.
Every dollar we raise goes straight to the work that wins elections:
* Supporting volunteers
* Printing materials
* Reaching voters in every community across the Commonwealth

[**This is our moment to finish strong so, can you donate $3 to our Super Saturday Fund to help us meet our $40,000 goal and help us reach every voter we need before Tuesday?**](https://act.virginiansforfairelections.com/go/41?c0=em_ms_vafe_20260418_2.S3qxwY&amount=3&refcode=20260418-533&t=2&akid=533%2E12592%2EI-rCvY)
**[Donate $3 ››](https://act.virginiansforfairelections.com/go/41?c0=em_ms_vafe_20260418_2.S3qxwY&amount=3&refcode=20260418-533&t=21&akid=533%2E12592%2EI-rCvY)**
[](https://act.virginiansforfairelections.com/go/1499?t=22&akid=533%2E12592%2EI-rCvY)
Let’s finish what we started,
Virginians for Fair Elections

[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_vafe_2026_footer?refcode=em&t=23&refcode2=533_12592_I-rCvY&akid=533%2E12592%2EI-rCvY)
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, so thank you for reading to the end.
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://vafe.actionkit.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=24&akid=533%2E12592%2EI-rCvY)

Contributions or gifts to Virginians for Fair Elections are not tax deductible.

subject: A lot of zeroes, Derek
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Mark Warner
body:
ADD YOUR NAME

|
---

| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/129719603/615307132/-672863980?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_PT_TrumpCorruptPT_260418_T1_1&ms=MW_ATA_EM_PT_TrumpCorruptPT_260418_T1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzODhlZDY3ZS0zYjNiLWYxMTEtOGVmMi0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=1snv-PJvqgCHB7NAHEG7dwbtypCriPa_j6RnFZ8huks=) Derek, I have an absolutely _outrageous_ number for you:

**In the first year of his second term, Donald Trump’s net worth grew by over $3,000,000,000 ($3 billion).**

That’s a lot of zeroes, Derek. And frankly, I can bet that virtually no one reading this email saw their net worth grow by that much last year.

Instead, I see families across Virginia and the country struggling under rising costs at the grocery store thanks to Trump’s illegal tariffs.

Gas prices are surging thanks to this president’s war of choice in the Middle East.

And millions are losing access to health care to pay for Trump’s tax breaks for the wealthiest Americans.

**Trump is using the presidency to enrich himself, his family, and his billionaire allies while working families get squeezed. It’s wrong.**

[_**The presidency should be about working for the people you were elected to serve – not making a quick buck (or billion). Can you sign your name and show that you oppose Trump’s corruption?**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/129719604/615307133/-599604168?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_PT_TrumpCorruptPT_260418_T1_1&ms=MW_ATA_EM_PT_TrumpCorruptPT_260418_T1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzODhlZDY3ZS0zYjNiLWYxMTEtOGVmMi0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=1snv-PJvqgCHB7NAHEG7dwbtypCriPa_j6RnFZ8huks=&emci=a399f47a-ca39-f111-8ef2-000d3a14b640&emdi=388ed67e-3b3b-f111-8ef2-000d3a14b640&ceid=4568237)

I’m running for reelection because I believe that government and public service can bring real change to people’s lives. That’s why I’m running to bring down costs, expand health care access, build more housing, and prepare our workforce for the changes brought on by Artificial Intelligence.

**And I’m running to take on this administration's blatant, self-serving corruption and continue holding Trump and his allies accountable whenever I can.**

I’m asking folks across the Commonwealth to stand with me, demand better, and work to elect Democrats across Virginia this fall who will put _you_ first, not themselves.

[_**Will you add your name to the growing list of grassroots supporters demanding transparency and accountability in Washington? >>**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/129719605/615307134/-599604168?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_PT_TrumpCorruptPT_260418_T1_1&ms=MW_ATA_EM_PT_TrumpCorruptPT_260418_T1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzODhlZDY3ZS0zYjNiLWYxMTEtOGVmMi0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=1snv-PJvqgCHB7NAHEG7dwbtypCriPa_j6RnFZ8huks=&emci=a399f47a-ca39-f111-8ef2-000d3a14b640&emdi=388ed67e-3b3b-f111-8ef2-000d3a14b640&ceid=4568237)

The fight continues,

Mark Warner **Before you go:**

We’re asking people like you – who care deeply about our democracy and the future of our country – to join us in our fight to help Democrats win the midterms and bring accountability and transparency to Washington.

[_**Will you contribute today to reelect Mark Warner, support Democrats in races across Virginia, and flip control of Congress?**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/129719606/615307135/-672863980?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_PT_TrumpCorruptPT_260418_T1_1&ms=MW_ATA_EM_PT_TrumpCorruptPT_260418_T1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzODhlZDY3ZS0zYjNiLWYxMTEtOGVmMi0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=1snv-PJvqgCHB7NAHEG7dwbtypCriPa_j6RnFZ8huks=)**__********[Contribute](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/129719607/615307136/-672863980?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_PT_TrumpCorruptPT_260418_T1_1&ms=MW_ATA_EM_PT_TrumpCorruptPT_260418_T1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzODhlZDY3ZS0zYjNiLWYxMTEtOGVmMi0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=1snv-PJvqgCHB7NAHEG7dwbtypCriPa_j6RnFZ8huks=)**********

clean_body: ADD YOUR NAME | | | = Derek, I have an absolutely outrageous number for you: In the first year of his second term, Donald Trump’s net worth grew by over $3,000,000,000 ($3 billion). That’s a lot of zeroes, Derek. And frankly, I can bet that virtually no one reading this email saw their net worth grow by that much last year. Instead, I see families across Virginia and the country struggling under rising costs at the grocery store thanks to Trump’s illegal tariffs. Gas prices are surging thanks to this president’s war of choice in the Middle East. And millions are losing access to health care to pay for Trump’s tax breaks for the wealthiest Americans. Trump is using the presidency to enrich himself, his family, and his billionaire allies while working families get squeezed. It’s wrong. I’m running for reelection because I believe that government and public service can bring real change to people’s lives. That’s why I’m running to bring down costs, expand health care access, build more housing, and prepare our workforce for the changes brought on by Artificial Intelligence. And I’m running to take on this administration's blatant, self-serving corruption and continue holding Trump and his allies accountable whenever I can. I’m asking folks across the Commonwealth to stand with me, demand better, and work to elect Democrats across Virginia this fall who will put you first, not themselves. The fight continues, Mark Warner Before you go: We’re asking people like you – who care deeply about our democracy and the future of our country – to join us in our fight to help Democrats win the midterms and bring accountability and transparency to Washington. =****** | | Friends of Mark Warner 1490-5A Quarterpath Road #213 Williamsburg, VA 23185 United States | Click here to . | Paid for by Friends of Mark Warner
